17545.text: @Daniel Arwe @Robert Hobbs @Victor Monterrey Testing note: inifnite countertops can be found in Countertops > fabricated countertops > Whole House Countertops.
@Daniel Arwe I have attached the SVG to this story.
@Victor Monterrey No test case needed. We'll test this with just a visual inspection

@Charlie Bradley III, could you create an SVG that looks like this image (transparent background, color agnostic)?  We want to be able to push any color to it.  I do not have a PNG for this.  The app. is using a base64 string as the image source, but we need it as an SVG so we can set the color according to builder theme.

@Robert Hobbs, I was able to work out the CSS for recreating the corner, 45 degree-rotated div, so we can theme that now....
       

Just need to know about the checkmark.

@Robert Hobbs, These are background images.  In order to make the color theme-able, we would need to create an SVG instead.  We already have another magnifying glass that looks the same, but is instead an SVG.  However, the current image includes the whole diagonal corner bit and not just the magnifying glass, so I would need to do some CSS work to recreate that 45 degree angled corner as a rotated <div>.  We would need to create a new SVG for the checkmark.  What would you like me to do?  Do these need to be theme-able?

@Daniel Arwe 

Yes, that seems like a decent approach. No further comment from me.

@Robert Hobbs, You mentioned wanting to know if the colors of the icons and text next to them could be linked.  You can do this by setting one CSS value to reference another CSS variable, so for example, the variable of the color next to the icon is --text-primary.  So we could just say something like --dmh-icon-color: --text-primary.  We have, in one area, where the text color is set to --text-primary, so we could do what I just mentioned, but since --text-primary is probably used in a whole bunch of other places, we might want to do something like...

--dmh-primary-text-icon-color: --text-primary
-dmh-room-card-icon-color: --dmh-primary-text-icon-color
--dmh-room-card-text-color: --dmh-primary-text-icon-color

That way, while the room card's icons and text are the same, and linked, they are not tied directly to --text-primary, and if future us wants to change those colors strictly in DMH, we can do so by changing the --dmh-primary-text-icon-color and not --text-primary (which likely affects a lot of views).  @Reid Wilson, please chime in on this, since you have more experience here and may be able to put your finger on important points that I might be missing.

16490.text: @Reid Wilson thanks for assembling this. From my pov, I think we need a work item to refactor selection to use a category id that corresponds to the lowest level of organization based on nesting depth. Another notion I had from your notes is whether it is feasible to support categories beyond 3 levels with a novel UI that increases depth but maintains the ability to easily go back up in the structure via navigational elements rather than trying to truncate or resize fonts etc.

Enhanced Product Categories: Architecture and Implementation Plan
  •  Roger produced a spike on this subject
    • the branch is rogerw/15023/category_sorting_spike
    • the branch can be compared to the closest release branch (6/10/24) to see what changes Roger actually made
    • Roger did not make a single reusable nav menu component; rather, he made changes in option pricing, design selections, and design my home
    • in each location, he modified the nav menu to get all of this working
      • the categories can infinitely nest
      • the categories can be given custom names from within VCMS
      • the categories can be given custom ordering from within VCMS
    • here is what is known to not work in Roger's spike
      • in OP/PC, selecting a non-estimated item in a nested category doesn't work
      • in DMH, clicking on a nested menu item always yields 0 results even though there should be results
      • in DS, clicking on a nested menu item always yields 0 results even though there should be results
    • I deployed the spike to reid.veodesignstudio.com for playtesting
  • Rob had a specific question about whether we should build a nav menu component to be used in 3 locations or implement enhanced categories in 3 locations separately
    • my answer is that we should implement enhanced categories in the 3 locations separately; the following is my reasoning
    • each location has minor differences from the others; these differences will need to be maintained in order to avoid any regressions; to do so with the nav menu component would require it to have a great deal of configuration settings; each location would configure the component's settings their own way which is fine, but when you start to realize that several settings seem "hacky" and would only work in 1 location and not another, then you know that you would just be better off creating 3 separate nav menus; one great example of this is eventing; we have specific events that get fired in each location, and it would be a pain (and hacky) to make configurations to fire and listen for particular ones
    • I reached out to Roger on this subject, and he said that in his spike, he originally tried to develop the single component; he encountered difficulties and due to time constraints, he ended up ditching the component and implementing manually in each location; it is worth noting that he did say a single component was doable, but you "will just need to consider how much gain it brings you"; in my educated opinion, it will be more struggle than gain to implement such a component
  • the plan
    • implement enhanced categories in option pricing / product catalog
      • use Roger's spike as a heavy guide
      • you will have to fix the selection issue
        • hint: the account_organization_user_profile_plan_options_budget_items table will have to be modified to support some kind of category id rather than using application name and product name
    • implement enhanced categories for design selections
    • implement enhanced categories for design my home
  • my recommendations
    • Rob's stories defined in the backlog (while not complete) are looking good
    • since it is infeasible to support nesting for infinite categories, I would recommend we restrict it to 3 (maybe just ignore data from VCMS that goes past 3); also, I recommend word-wrapping category names and truncating past a certain point if necessary rather than, per say, dynamically decreasing font size, dynamically extending the width of nav menu, etc.

@<5665B600-D2CF-6DDE-A026-6CE84CEA59C5> , @<D2E79038-97CA-6D63-8A21-A92E9A844981> , @<DED1F3BE-FA1D-6B76-B3AE-F3AE291DCC5F>  **Courtesy of ChatGPT.  Pasting this here for memory jogging/knowledge sharing purposes when it comes time to build the VDS and VEO Admin frontends that interact with this backend implementation.**

Frontend Integration Notes
==========================

*   **GET** should be used to render UI with a list of options, using:
    *   `id` as the stable identifier
        
    *   `lookupKey`, `name`, `type` as descriptive metadata
        
    *   `value` as the display/edit value
        
    *   `isValueDefault` to show “default vs overridden” state in the UI
        GetSelectionsReportOptions
        
*   **SAVE** expects a batch of `{ reportOptionId, value }` pairs. Values are validated and normalized server-side before persistence. If the UI submits multiple entries for the same `reportOptionId`, it will hard-fail with 400.
    SaveSelectionsReportOptions
    
*   The save response provides a **per-option status** (`CREATED`, `UPDATED`, `NOT_MODIFIED`) that can be used for toast messages or audit displays.
    SaveSelectionsReportOptionOverr…

@<4C46EFE8-26F9-6F48-B257-971123DDB984> Courtesy of ChatGPT:

QA Test Scenarios
=================

GET /api/selectionsReportOption/{accountId}/{organizationId}
------------------------------------------------------------

1.  **Happy path — no overrides**
    *   Preconditions: defaults exist; no overrides exist for the account/org
        
    *   Expected: returns list where `value == default.Value` and `isValueDefault == true` for all items
        GetSelectionsReportOptions
        
2.  **Happy path — with overrides**
    *   Preconditions: at least one override exists for account/org
        
    *   Expected: that option’s `value == override.Value` and `isValueDefault == false` unless override equals default
        GetSelectionsReportOptions
        
3.  **404 when account not found**
    *   Expected: 404 with message: `Account with account_id: {id} not found.`
        GetSelectionsReportOptions
        
4.  **404 when organization not found**
    *   Expected: 404 with message: `Organization with organization_id: {id} not found.`
        GetSelectionsReportOptions
        

* * *

POST /api/selectionsReportOption/save
-------------------------------------

1.  **Create new override**
    *   Preconditions: default exists; no existing override for that reportOptionId
        
    *   Expected:
        *   result item has `previousValue = null`
            
        *   `status = CREATED`
            SaveSelectionsReportOptionOverr…
            
2.  **Update existing override**
    *   Preconditions: override exists with a different value than incoming
        
    *   Expected:
        *   `previousValue` is old override value
            
        *   `status = UPDATED`
            SaveSelectionsReportOptions
            SaveSelectionsReportOptionOverr…
            
3.  **No change (same value)**
    *   Preconditions: override exists and incoming value normalizes to same stored value
        
    *   Expected:
        *   `status = NOT_MODIFIED`
            SaveSelectionsReportOptionOverr…
            
    *   Note: The code still sets `ModifiedDate`/`Modifier` on “existing override” path even if unchanged (but status will show NOT_MODIFIED).
        SaveSelectionsReportOptions
        
4.  **Duplicate ReportOptionId in request**
    *   Input: same `reportOptionId` appears twice in `selectionsReportOptions`
        
    *   Expected: `400 Bad Request` with message like:  
        `Duplicate ReportOptionId: {id} found in request. No duplicates allowed.`
        SaveSelectionsReportOptions
        SelectionsReportOptionController
        
5.  **Invalid ReportOptionId (not found in defaults)**
    *   Input: a `reportOptionId` GUID not present in `SelectionsReportOptionDefault`
        
    *   Expected: `404 Not Found` with message:  
        `SelectionsReportOptionDefault with ReportOptionId: {id} not found.`
        SaveSelectionsReportOptions
        SelectionsReportOptionController
        
6.  **Invalid value for type**
    *   Input: provide a value that fails `SelectionsReportOptionTypeValueHelper.ValidateAndNormalizeValueByType(...)`
        
    *   Expected: `400 Bad Request` with message:  
        `Invalid value: '{value}' for '{defaultOption.Name}' (ReportOptionId: {id}) of type: {Type}.`
        SaveSelectionsReportOptions
        SelectionsReportOptionController
        
7.  **404 when account not found**
    *   Expected: 404 with message:  
        `Account with account_id: {id} not found.`
        SaveSelectionsReportOptions
        
8.  **404 when organization not found**
    *   Expected: 404 with message:  
        `Organization with organization_id: {id} not found.`
        SaveSelectionsReportOptions
        (via ValidateOrganization path in save use case)
        SaveSelectionsReportOptions

@<4C46EFE8-26F9-6F48-B257-971123DDB984> , @<6E668580-F8AB-64C3-A4C1-1DDE45503DA2> Courtesy of ChatGPT:

Selections Report Options API (Backend)
=======================================

Overview
--------

These endpoints support reading and saving “Selections Report Options” for a specific **Account + Organization** pair.
*   Defaults are stored in `SelectionsReportOptionDefault`
    
*   Overrides are stored in `SelectionsReportOptionOverride`
    
*   **GET** returns defaults merged with any overrides
    
*   **POST save** upserts overrides for the supplied option IDs and returns per-option results (created/updated/not_modified)
    
Controller base route:  
`/api/selectionsReportOption`
SelectionsReportOptionController

* * *

Endpoint 1 — Get selections report options
------------------------------------------

### Route

`GET /api/selectionsReportOption/{accountId}/{organizationId}`
SelectionsReportOptionController

### Auth

Uses the controller’s `SecurityToken` (from `BaseApiController`) and passes it into the use case.
SelectionsReportOptionController
GetSelectionsReportOptions

### Behavior

*   Validates **Account exists** for `accountId` + `securityToken`
    
*   Validates **Organization exists** for `organizationId` + `securityToken`
    
*   Loads all defaults
    
*   Loads overrides for the (accountId, organizationId) pair
    
*   Returns `SelectionsReportOptionDTO[]` where each row is a default option merged with an override (if present)
    GetSelectionsReportOptions
    

### Response DTO: `SelectionsReportOptionDTO`

`{   "id": "GUID",   "lookupKey": "string",   "name": "string",   "type": "string",   "value": "string",   "isValueDefault": true }`

Fields: `Id`, `LookupKey`, `Name`, `Type`, `Value`, `IsValueDefault`
SelectionsReportOptionDTO

### Merge Rules (Default vs Override)

For each default option:
*   `value` = override value if an override exists; otherwise default value
    
*   `isValueDefault` = `true` if no override exists **OR** override value equals default value
    GetSelectionsReportOptions
    

### Status Codes / Errors

*   `200 OK`: Returns merged list
    
*   `404 Not Found`: If Account not found or Organization not found  
    Controller **intentionally bypasses** `BaseApiController.NotFound` because it returns 400; this endpoint returns a true 404.
    SelectionsReportOptionController
    GetSelectionsReportOptions
    

### Example Response

`[   {     "id": "11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111",     "lookupKey": "SomeKey",     "name": "Include Pricing",     "type": "Boolean",     "value": "true",     "isValueDefault": false   } ]`

* * *

Endpoint 2 — Save selections report options
-------------------------------------------

### Route

`POST /api/selectionsReportOption/save`
SelectionsReportOptionController

### Auth

Uses the controller’s `SecurityToken` and passes it into the save use case.
SelectionsReportOptionController
SaveSelectionsReportOptions

### Request DTO: `SaveSelectionsReportOptionsRequest`

`{   "accountId": "GUID",   "organizationId": "GUID",   "selectionsReportOptions": [     {       "reportOptionId": "GUID",       "value": "string"     }   ] }`

SaveSelectionsReportOptionsRequ…
SaveSelectionsReportOptionDTO

### Response DTO: `SaveSelectionsReportOptionOverrideResult[]`

Each element describes the result of upserting a single option override.

`{   "reportOptionId": "GUID",   "name": "string",   "previousValue": "string|null",   "value": "string",   "status": "CREATED|UPDATED|NOT_MODIFIED" }`

*   `status` is computed:
    *   `CREATED` when `PreviousValue` is null
        
    *   `UPDATED` when `PreviousValue != Value`
        
    *   `NOT_MODIFIED` when `PreviousValue == Value`
        SaveSelectionsReportOptionOverr…
        

### Save / Upsert Rules

For each item in `selectionsReportOptions`:
1.  Validate account exists, org exists
    
2.  Load all defaults and build a lookup of defaults by `ReportOptionId` (which is `SelectionsReportOptionDefault.Id`)
    SaveSelectionsReportOptions
    
3.  Validate:
    *   Every `ReportOptionId` in request exists in defaults (else NotFound)
        SaveSelectionsReportOptions
        
    *   No duplicate `ReportOptionId` values in the request (else InvalidOperationException)
        SaveSelectionsReportOptions
        
    *   Every value is valid for the default’s type; values are normalized and written back into the request before saving (else InvalidOperationException)
        SaveSelectionsReportOptions
        
4.  Upsert behavior:
    *   If override exists for (AccountId, OrganizationId, ReportOptionId): update `Value`, `Modifier`, `ModifiedDate`
        
    *   Else create a new override row with provided value
        SaveSelectionsReportOptions
        
5.  Save changes once at the end, return the result list.
    SaveSelectionsReportOptions
    

### Status Codes / Errors

*   `200 OK`: Returns `SaveSelectionsReportOptionOverrideResult[]`
    SelectionsReportOptionController
    
*   `404 Not Found`: If account not found, org not found, or any supplied `ReportOptionId` not found in defaults  
    (controller intentionally returns 404 directly)
    SelectionsReportOptionController
    SaveSelectionsReportOptions
    
*   `400 Bad Request`: For invalid operations:
    *   Duplicate ReportOptionId in request
        
    *   Invalid value for option type (after validation/normalization step)
        SelectionsReportOptionController
        SaveSelectionsReportOptions
        

### Example Request

`{   "accountId": "aaaaaaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaaaaaaaaaa",   "organizationId": "bbbbbbbb-bbbb-bbbb-bbbb-bbbbbbbbbbbb",   "selectionsReportOptions": [     {       "reportOptionId": "11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111",       "value": "true"     }   ] }`

### Example Response

`[   {     "reportOptionId": "11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111",     "name": "Include Pricing",     "previousValue": "false",     "value": "true",     "status": "UPDATED"   } ]`

To better work across teams, I added the same tasks from this story to a Planner board that is in the BOT Communication Teams channel. I needed a company-wide Team so Dorliza and Aaron could view it. 

Go to the team > General Communication > Along top row select Historymaker Theme.

The tasks are organized by Design To Do, Developer To Do, Ready for QA, Done and Will Not Do.

Feel free to adjust the board if there is a better way to organize this.
Dorliza requested today for the ability to change the image in Product Performace & Care

You may have noticed we have updated the name of this theme. The business has said that History Maker is a higher priority for them over stylecraft. I checked in with folks and most said that no real variable defining was done that would be lost by a change.

My ideas for improvements:
1) The way vds calls for variable values and how that is implemented can be refined. The current endpoint that gets the theme variables also returns the dashboard images. That enpoint needs to be split up and would be able to be more effeceint. When get variable values, we stuff all the variables into one style element dynamically. We should build style elements based on how the themes layer on each other.
2) Default should not be changed ever and specifically live in code. The _variable.scss file should be the master copy of default. We should then create a process for the site to be able to read the variables from _variable.scss and then 1- confirm that the variables presisted in the database are correct 2- the variable values for default are updated correctly 3- if changes are needed notify someone of the changes. This idea will make sure that there is one source of default and it is not deviating, keep our variables in line and if worst case scenario happens the site can repopulate sql.

From todays work with Dorliza, the following comments were mentioned:
  • It was a very hard process for her know where the variable values changed for certain things
    • we need a way to communicate what the variables do. We have discussed different ideas.
  • Would be nice to link css pointers and navigate to that variable definition.
    • I believe more information is needed for this before we take action on this. I beleive she wants to have 'aliasing' or a specific pallete and drop theme into the CSS variables to define them.
  • Would be nice to organize the variables with the pages they are apart of.
    • Rob floated out the tagging method. Could just a different way to organize the variables
  • Be able to change the images that are utilized in the site
    • Currently the following variables are comprised of urls for default:
      bg-ds-leftPane
      bg-dmh-topBar
      bg-dmh
      bg-dmh-right
      bg-lifestyle
      bg-ds-mobileMenu
      bg-optionPricing-leftPane
      bg-dmh-left
      bg-ds-mobileMenuLauncher-button
      bg-dark
    • Hot Idea: For themes create a new category as a 'bucket' to upload images to be utilized in the variables. Then when editing the variables have a way to let the users be able to get the url of the image that would be utilizing our endpoints into the variable edit.
  • She wanted more control over the text properties within specific places in our app.
    • We have a story in the pipe
    • we should formalize a rule of thumb of what a user can touch and modify

Should comparison of Name, for uniqueness be case-sensitve or case-insensitive?  
For example, would we allow 'Theme1' and 'theme1' to coexist? My vote would be 'NO', however, the current implementation (as of 2:32pm today) is that the comparison ignores casing when determining uniqueness, which allows both to coexist.

Based on our UI/UX conversation earlier today, an example of how Vis. Program create is handling error display....


Do we want to do something similar for create Theme?  

Duplicate name is the only error that cannot be fully prevented in the frontend, since there is the possibility that two users create a Theme with the same name, one right after the other, such that the frontend does not have the most recent knowledge of existing theme names on Save.

For name and description character count constraints, with Vis. Programs, we are preventing with maxLength attribute on the input, so errors of this type never surface from the backend unless bypassing the UI and hitting the endpoint directly (such as via Postman).  IMHO, we could do the same thing here.  If we want to give the user extra feedback, could put a character count on the input, but such an implementation is not used anywhere else in VEO Admin (to my knowledge).

@Daniel Arwe @Charlie Bradley III My thought is to create an SVG for the VDS Logo if we have time for that. It would need to be done today or tomorrow latest I think to hit our target though

@Robert Hobbs, @Charlie Bradley III One problem we are going to encounter is that the VDS logo is an image and not an SVG, so our ability to dynamically set or theme the color is limited.  I like the mockups using a light header background and darker logo, with one of TM's colors, however we currently have a few problems with implementation if we leave the logo as an image.  Currently, we have two versions of the VDS logo....
  1. vds-logo.png (white text, with the exception of the 'VEO' part, which have a wavy pool-water-like background)
  2. vds-logo-for-dark-bg.png (white text throughout)
  3. vds-logo-for-white-bg (white text, with the exception of the 'VEO' part, which have a wavy pool-water-like background)
Currently, there isn't a great way to support dynamically setting the background image, other than setting it in the JS/Typescript by saying "If the theme is a, b, or c, use the logo for white background....else, use the logo for dark background," which adds the need for the JS/TS code to have direct knowledge of each theme, rather than it being data-driven.  I don't know how far that logic would need to be propogated in the frontend code.

All that to say, I see several approaches to addressing the issue:
  1. Make the logo an SVG, thus making it themeable.
  2. Add backend and/or data-persistence logic and/or properties to allow rendering of a specific VDS logo image, depending on the theme
  3. As a stopgap for this story, use a darker background for the header with the logo image for dark background, like this...

#3 above presents the problem of a light-background TM logo (unlike the Staggered logo above) against the dark header background.  Currently, the logo for white background (I think) is only getting used in email templates, with the site having the logo for dark background hard-coded, so the SVG (at least for use in the header) may be our best best and would allow use to make the VDS logo any color we want, and theme it.  I like @Charlie Bradley III's light header with 'VEO DESIGN STUDIO'  in the TM dark taupe color shown in the mockups, but that might be acheivable most easily if we have an SVG logo.

Thoughts??

@Jennifer Hickey @Walter Martinez @Robert Hobbs @Daniel Arwe

Here is the best way I can describe the data that must be in place in order to reproduce both bugs (from History Maker and from this one at Toll Brothers). I will describe 2 cases that would cause a problem at some point in the past. These 2 cases can be tested both at once or separately - doesn't matter; they would cause a bug in the past either way.

Note: The grid is always composed of a number of areas (rows) and a number of price levels (columns). The areas are always sorted alphabetically.

Case 1 (HistoryMaker bug):
There must exist an area X with at least 2 price levels available. There must also exist an area Y with fewer price levels available than area X. There must also exist a price level that is available for area Y that is not available for area X. The price level which area Y has for which area X does not would not show up as a column, but it should.

Case 2 (Toll Brothers bug):
There must exist an area Y that exists alphabetically below (after) an area X. Area Y must have an available price level, call it A, which area X does not have available. This price level A must have a higher (earlier) sort order than the leftmost price level column. Instead of correctly appearing to the left of the leftmost column, price level A appears to the right of all of the price levels which area X has. This causes the noticeable "shift" bug for area Y. How do we know the sort order? We have attached a script to this story. You can run it to see all of the possible price levels sorted correctly, given a particular application and product (the script might default to Countertops and Fabricated Ctops because it is that combo which caused all of these bugs). The deciding factor for the sorting is the contract sort order field. The price level sorting moves from least contract sort order to greatest contract sort order (it is an integer). In the event there is a tie, the price level name is used to sort alphabetically ascending. Note also that just because a price level appears in the output of the script does not mean it should appear in the UI. You should only check what you see in the UI and compare the ordering against what you see in the output of the script.

Tip for Case 2: It might be a little overwhelming to come up with areas X and Y and price level A that would meet the criteria exactly. I can tell you right now that going to Countertops --> Fabricated Ctops in non-prod environments will satisfy the criteria. The "Bath 2 Pony Wall" can be area X. The "Bath 2 Vanity" can be area Y. The "Specialty Stone - Level 1" can be price level A.

Let me know if there are any questions at all about this, and we will answer them. Thanks.

@Walter Martinez, @Reid Wilson, @Jennifer Hickey, @Robert Hobbs  Repro. info. from Slack thread earlier....


For an Application > Product (Example: Countertops > Fabricated Ctops)....
{Example: DEV > Staggered > LOPF > Series 1 > Aransas} =>
  • Need 1 area that
  • Has fewer price levels than the area with the most price levels
  • "Bath 2 Vanity" has the most price levels (14); other areas all have 13, except "Kitchen w/Range" (12 price levels) and "Utility Countertop" (2 price levels)
  • Specialty Stone - Level 1
  • ************************MISSING Specialty Stone - Level 2 (which "Utility Countertop" area HAS)
  • Marlana - Level 1
  • Marlana - Level 2
  • Granite - Level 1
  • Granite - Level 2
  • Granite - Level 3
  • Granite - Level 4
  • Granite - Level 5
  • Granite - Level 6
  • Artisan Granite - Level 1
  • Artisan Granite - Level 2
  • Artisan Granite - Level 3
  • Artisan Granite - Level 4
  • Artisan Granite - Level 5
  • Has a price level that the area with the most price levels does not have
  • Note that "Kitchen w/Range", with 13 price levels, has the following price levels....but it displays as expected, since none of its price levels are NOT present in "Bath 2 Vanity", the area with the most price levels (14).
  • Specialty Stone - Level 1
  • Marlana - Level 1
  • Marlana - Level 2
  • Granite - Level 1
  • Granite - Level 2
  • ************************MISSING Granite - Level 3
  • Granite - Level 4
  • Granite - Level 5
  • Granite - Level 6
  • Artisan Granite - Level 1
  • Artisan Granite - Level 2
  • Artisan Granite - Level 3
  • Artisan Granite - Level 4
  • Artisan Granite - Level 5
  • Note also that "Utility Countertop" has only 2 price levels....one of them (Specialty Stone - Level 1) is present in the 14 price levels for "Bath 2 Vanity".  However, one of them (Specialty Stone - Level 2) is NOT present in the 14 price levels for "Bath 2 Vanity".
  • Specialty Stone - Level 1 (present in "Bath 2 Vanity" 14 price levels)
  • Specialty Stone - Level 2 (NOT present in "Bath 2 Vanity" 14 price levels)
  • SO......in the currently deployed code, when the UI builds out the rows for each area it....
  • Grabs the area with the most price levels (14, in the example above)
  • Iterates over areas....for each area....
  • Iterates over the price levels (14) and....
  • for any price level that the current area being iterated does not include, insert (splice) in "Unavailable"
  • This is significant because....for the "Utility Countertop" area....this iteration process shakes out as follows:
  • Utility Countertop has....(note the 13 Unavailable prices, corresponding to the 13 price levels "Bath 2 Vanity" has, but "Utility Countertop" doesn't and the absence of "Specialty Stone - Level 2", which the area "Utility Countertop" has, but the list of 14 price levels derived from "Bath 2 Vanity" does not.  This missing price level is not accounted for in the iteration, but the price level still exists in "Utility Countertop" area.  This is the reason for the last $0 price "hanging" off the end of the row with no column header.
  • Specialty Stone - Level 1 (TRUE) => $0
  • Marlana - Level 1 (FALSE) => Unavailable
  • Marlana - Level 2 (FALSE) => Unavailable
  • Granite - Level 1 (FALSE) => Unavailable
  • Granite - Level 2 (FALSE) => Unavailable
  • Granite - Level 3 (FALSE) => Unavailable
  • Granite - Level 4 (FALSE) => Unavailable
  • Granite - Level 5 (FALSE) => Unavailable
  • Granite - Level 6 (FALSE) => Unavailable
  • Artisan Granite - Level 1 (FALSE) => Unavailable
  • Artisan Granite - Level 2 (FALSE) => Unavailable
  • Artisan Granite - Level 3 (FALSE) => Unavailable
  • Artisan Granite - Level 4 (FALSE) => Unavailable
  • Artisan Granite - Level 5 (FALSE) => Unavailable
  • Therefore, the row for the "Utility Countertop" area is expanded, via the iteration above, to 15 total columns, but there are only headers for 14 of those columns.
  • Now for the fix....the new code, not yet pushed to DEV,
  • creates a master list of price levels, to encompass ALL distinct price levels from ALL areas for an Application > Product group,
  • and takes special care to order them according to the sorting logic already utilized in the backend code that was already sending the areas back.
  • It was this ordering that was not accounted for in the previous History Maker "fix", which broke Toll Brothers' OP functionality
  • The new implementation, taking into account the ordering, should provide a fix for both.

22878.text:
@<6E668580-F8AB-64C3-A4C1-1DDE45503DA2> This issue was caused by calling .toISOString() on the startDate and endDate, which does not inherently correct for the date-time offset based on local timezone.  This was pushing the startDate and endDate for the parameters sent to the backend to get pushed 6 hours later, which is why an endDate of 03-31-2025 was also pulling in some 04-01-2025 records.  The new implementation applies this offset.

![image.png](https://dev.azure.com/BuildOnTechnologies/ddde192e-e31e-4e51-af4b-3b7b4221266a/_apis/wit/attachments/7397210a-60e2-4fa8-8ec5-91f641b86b2b?fileName=image.png)

20314.text:
Most likely, this issue was caused by an update to the Microsoft.Data.SqlClient library, which now turns on encryption by default. This is a breaking change, requiring us to update our connection strings to either turn off encryption (bad idea) or trust the certificate (better idea). I've put together a quick blog post about this issue, which you can read about here: https://buildontechnologies.sharepoint.com/:u:/r/sites/SystemsTeam/SitePages/SQL-Server-Connection-Encryption.aspx?csf=1&web=1&e=yqG28z.

I also asked Jennifer to test this issue again in the Roger environment and she can no longer reproduce the issue. I checked the Roger environment and it has the "TrustServerCertificate=true;" parameter added to all of its connection strings. We will need to update all of the other environments before deploying the .NET 9 changes, to keep them working.

@Robert Hobbs I have not reachout yet to talk through possible implementation strategies
@Justin Pope did we reach out to Areas concerning the number 2 option below?

Enhancement to Vignettes:

*** Have multiple vignette scenes loaded at the same time ***

VDS Approach
    Without the assistance of Aareas, we could implement within DMH visualization for non-"whole house" and vignettes to leverage multiple streamURLs for each indiviual scene within multiple iFrames. DMH would then bubble up the correct iFrame based on the room that the user is in. Ideally the iFrames would all load once at the same time and then would have "apply material" messages posted to apply selections.
   This approach would take a spike to design / configure and most likely a follow up story to implement.

VDS and Aareas Approach
   Design a system with Aareas to descrease the amount of "load time" for scenes in visualization. The starting concept would flow similar too:
1) request to Aareas to initialize the individual scenes and Aareas give us individual streams per scene
2) DMH switches between the streams per room
Side note, that selection could be posted to Aareas and the selections are applied to each individual stream.
    This would most likely be the following:
        1) Design story with Aareas to create a system
        2) Spike to implement the concepts created from design, also depended on how quick Aareas can implement said system
        3) based on success of spike, implement to production in sperate story

21153.text:
@Jennifer Hickey @Charlie Bradley III @Reid Wilson @Daniel Arwe @Joe Ebeling @Walter Martinez @Eric Hickey 

Jennifer, I think this is a good question. I will try to clarify my original intent. 

The groups and relationships should be global and for the most part a single source of truth based on our latest representation of how to theme the site. Right now it's imperfect (hence our conversations yesterday) and we want to go through a design / aesthetic effort (along with new variable creation) move to something more intuitive / simpler for everyone involved. 

So my original intent with the script was to be able to say "ok it makes more sense to have a group called 'Color 4' that gets applied to the title bar and these other locations in these modules. Let's create that new group and shift all those css names from the groups they are in over into Color 4"  In my mind I was thinking of this script as a "seed" script that re-writes all those relationships and it represents the latest and best form of those relationships and once we are happy with a change we apply it to all environments. 

When applying it to production, there is a bit more that has to be done because it may have the potential to change the way an existing theme appears the next time a support user changes a group (palette) variable value. So some communication with those who maintain the themes would be created similar to the release notes communication. "Hey just letting you know we moved these variables x, y, z from the group they are in into a new group..." something like this. 

I want to say that when I wrote this the intent was simply that I recognized the current relationships need further refinement and I wanted a way to update them easily without a code deployment. Also I should say that I was never thinking I would be the only person doing this, but rather it would be either myself (if others were not so engaged) and/or design folks or the team generally. 

To your point about testing - other than just trying something out (experimenting) with how it feels in the editor I would not expect us to have wildly different group relationships across the different environments. My idea with the script was to have the original one that seeds the relationships and then experiment with refactoring that and then once we agree that it's better apply it everywhere as the latest version. 

This is not very structured, I realize. Having the latest version be a script (not in a repo) is a bit dangerous but having the data in the database be the only representation (even in QA) is more so in my mind. So a happy medium might be a repo file for the relationships but one where it's mostly to keep history not to rely upon it for deployments, as I don't want us to be limited to a code release to update these group mappings. 

One other point about them changing - they are guaranteed to change over time even if we think they are perfect because we will be adding new views / dialogs etc. 

Anyway, hope that helps convey what I was originally thinking on the topic. Let me know if you have some further questions.

@Robert Hobbs What are your plans with this script? Just you making changes to Preview? You'll be using it to make changes to Staging and Prod as well? Other people will be able to request changes anytime and they will be made?

After this script is run on any Stack in any enviornment, that stack/enviornment obviously will no longer have the same parent/child groups as current. Even within an environment, stacks can differ. QA will no longer be used as the "this is what everywhere visually looks like" test. What we expect in one environment will be different than every other environment/stack. Over time, as these groupings deviate, testing new features across environments/stacks will become more time consuming bc we'll be dealing with grouping differences on top of normal functionality testing. These new groupings will need to re-evaluated each for each test as they will be changing.

For example, we now test in QA>Staggered. If text is aligned, fits, can be seen, etc then we know that except for text length due to data, it will look the same all the way to Prod. This script changes that. A font grouped as normal in QA could be grouped as bold in staging or prod and the UI no longer works. QA will need to test each possibility QA to find these issues. This script, and the possibilities it allows, opens the can of worms that will increase the QA effort on any user story/bug with a UI component.

Tagging the team as this will come up in backlog refinements @Charlie Bradley III @Reid Wilson @Daniel Arwe @Joe Ebeling  @Walter Martinez @Eric Hickey

20311.text:
Most likely, this issue was caused by an update to the Microsoft.Data.SqlClient library, which now turns on encryption by default. This is a breaking change, requiring us to update our connection strings to either turn off encryption (bad idea) or trust the certificate (better idea). I've put together a quick blog post about this issue, which you can read about here: https://buildontechnologies.sharepoint.com/:u:/r/sites/SystemsTeam/SitePages/SQL-Server-Connection-Encryption.aspx?csf=1&web=1&e=yqG28z.

I also asked Jennifer to test this issue again in the Roger environment and she can no longer reproduce the issue. I checked the Roger environment and it has the "TrustServerCertificate=true;" parameter added to all of its connection strings. We will need to update all of the other environments before deploying the .NET 9 changes, to keep them working.

17814.text:
@Reid Wilson @Daniel Arwe @Jennifer Hickey @Walter Martinez @Victor Monterrey

All good on the review. Out of curiosity though should a local storage entry with my own user ID and a value for the org that I want to use also work for setting the default builder on login? I attempted to do that and it doesn't appear to work but I wasn't sure from the description if that would work for a sys-admin as well.

✅QA Validation Completed! 

PFA A quick video where it shows that the main issue was successfully solved.

@Reid Wilson @Jennifer Hickey @Walter Martinez @Victor Monterrey hey Reid, thanks for documenting this. As an approach to the fix, I think this is good. I am hoping logic-wise we are still solid for home buyers. Buyers will mostly be 1 org / with 1-2 plans. As we grow into more communities, more buyers will be multiple builders, multiple plans (prospective) and contracted 1 plan. Then repeat buyers will be one of those two scenarios and with a more recent plan / contracted plan. I think for buyers having this resolve with logic makes sense if possible to minimize their decision making and getting in a bad spot. 

For the other user roles such as Sales, Designers and Sys Admins the scenarios are different. Sales will typically have 1 builder but numerous plans. For them it's not a huge problem since the builder they work for should always be the default. For Designers in DCs they have multiple builders and multiple plans and so impersonation of buyers is probably the most common scenario for them, though I guess they do design work for spec homes also, so they may just be switching builders a lot. For Sys-Admins we just have no way to designate it and the logic seems to be hostile toward us! 😊In my case, I don't want to designate a default plan, but a default org. So for us I think we will need something different as a solution. 

My thoughts right now is that perhaps for non-buyers we plan out some kind of preferences dialog for a user where we store user settings and somehow make it easier for them to designate this kind of behavior as well as others. I don't think we have a uniform data structure for this yet (surprisingly).

@Robert Hobbs @Jennifer Hickey @Walter Martinez @Victor Monterrey

Here is an explanation of the logic I found.

The app will check if the logged in user has a "default profile plan". I would rather not have to type out all of the logic behind the algorithm which calculates it because it is extensive. But I know homebuyers will always have a default profile plan and Sys-Admins will not. Sales counselors probably don't, but don't quote me on that. If the default profile plan is not null, then the default account/org will be the account/org of the default profile plan. However, if the default profile plan is null, then the first org under the first account will be used as the default account/org (this is indeterministic). The default org star is ignored entirely.

The purpose of this story as defined in the repro steps and AC is to fix the default org star and report on the default builder logic and how best to implement an intentional builder for sys-admin users. Allow me to explain my current fix for the org star.

Before making changes, I could see that when you select the star, a local storage setting is applied to the user's browser with the key "veo.defaultOrg." + userID. Then this key was for some reason only being used to aid in the default profile plan calculation and ignored if there would be no default profile plan. My fix is to still check for default profile plan just as before and use that account/org if found, but then if no default profile plan, check for this default org star local storage setting and apply account/org from it if found, and only if no default org star either, then use the first org under the first account. This patches the org star bug without changing any other existing functionality. Note that if a sales counselor hits the star for an org in one browser and then moves to another browser, the default org would not respect the star selection from the prior browser due to this being a local storage setting.

Regarding my thoughts on how to best implement an intentional default builder for sys-admin users, I think that is first and foremost a UI/UX question and I don't think I should comment on what UI we should add to set the default account/org for sys-admins. However, I will say that my planned fix will work for whatever UI/UX solution we decide on because all that will have to happen behind the scenes is that local storage setting getting updated from the new UI; the logic to determine default account/org will not have to be changed. If, on the other hand, you hate the idea of this being a local storage setting, I would vote for a separate story to store some kind of record in the DB. Let me know if you like my fix or otherwise how best to proceed. Thanks.

@<6E668580-F8AB-64C3-A4C1-1DDE45503DA2> , @<4C46EFE8-26F9-6F48-B257-971123DDB984> , @<16695B85-2F2C-6860-B106-056F5D5396E1> In the story discussion, there was a question of whether we can distinguish between two browser tabs in which the same user logs into VDS.  The answer is yes, based on the Security Token and the timestamp extracted from the JWT token.  To make things simpler, the response for the authenticate endpoint already returns the Security Token separately (GUID), so we could just use that to determine that separate tabs/windows were used by the same user.  Here is a breakdown of the differences in the JWT token according to chat.

![==image_0==.png](https://dev.azure.com/BuildOnTechnologies/ddde192e-e31e-4e51-af4b-3b7b4221266a/_apis/wit/attachments/f6c1f87b-a753-4ef8-b2e2-cd07b09b6be2?fileName===image_0==.png)

**IF THERE'S EXTRA TIME....**

If a user has multiple VDS tabs open simultaneouly, multiple events will be recorded for each tab.  Is there a way to determine some sort of unique identifier for each tab or app. instance, that we could log in order to de-obfuscate the data? Perhaps a user token?

An initial heartbeat event should fire as soon as the timer starts.

Also, no tracking until user logs in.
Hearbeat and module change events fire independent of each other.  So, if a user has a heartbeat fire and 5 seconds later they navigate away, the timing of the next heartbeat event is not affected.

Common user flows to extrapolate average time on site and number of events generated were discussed in pre-story meeting.

20384.text:
@Robert Hobbs

STORY DELIVERABLES

  • Discussion on single font property vs multiple font properties:
    • Allowing the support user to set font weights gives him or her the ability to hose the site but also to fix it when it gets hosed. This is acceptable as long as their is a legitimate use case for it since there are a million other ways we allow support users to hose the site. However, we agreed there was not a legitimate use case for setting an overarching font weight. For the sake of knowledge-sharing, let's run through what Reid was thinking about if we did decide to allow setting font weight. If we allowed setting the font in short-hand mode (1 property), it would become impossible to both respect the designated font weight and also respect the font weights designated in 4 central CSS classes named intuitively "fontMainLight", "fontMainRegular", "fontMainItalic", and "fontMainBold" found in _typography.scss. The HTML across hundreds of views uses these classes. Whether text appears as bold, light, regular, or italic has meaning and such meaning would be wiped away if we simply let the support user dictate a specific font weight across the site. Note that Rob did mention a possibility in the future of multiple fonts. This is a reasonable idea which I have no problem with, but it does increase complexity. It will need to be thoughtfully handled regarding which locations are forced to use "font-secondary" per say or if we would allow a location to variably decide which font to use. It is also worth mentioning the font variant property which has 2 values: normal and small-caps. In the future, we could create a CSS class called "fontSmallCaps" which simply toggles on small-caps. Without the CSS class used, normal mode is used by default.
  • List of web-safe fonts that should be supported and at minimum work on Reid's machine:
    • Arial
    • Verdana
    • Tahoma
    • Trebuchet MS
    • Times New Roman
    • Georgia
    • Garamond
    • Courier New
    • Brush Script MT
  • Guidance on how to develop support for downloaded fonts:
    • Note that this method will require a re-deployment; I will be looking into how to do something similar without a re-deployment
    • Place the downloaded font file in the fonts folder of the web app
    • Create a new file called _localFonts.scss under the base style folder
    • Import this file in the main.scss file as the first import for the base category
    • Inside _localFonts.scss, add an @font-face rule for each font import like so:
      • @font-face {
            font-family: 'Neuropolitical';
            src: url(../../../Content/fonts/Neuropolitical.otf) format('opentype');
            font-weight: normal;
            font-style: normal;
        }
    • Note that other file types exist such as woff and woff2; woff2 is actually the recommended one
  • Guidance on how to test that a target font was actually imported successfully rather than your computer or browser coincidentally having support for it:
    • First, get information from a knowledgeable developer how the target font is meant to be imported. There are 3 general possibilities: web-safe font (no import necessary), import via CDN (ex. Google, Adobe, etc.), or import via downloaded file.
    • If the target font is considered a web-safe font, follow these steps. Open your Windows Control Panel. Search "Font". Click "View Installed Fonts". You should see the target font included here. Note that on Reid's machine "Brush Script MT" is installed as "Brush Script MT Italic" but Reid thinks it must be equivalent because it worked. But every other font should be named exactly the same as the list above. If the font is included here, your browser should display it properly in our web app.
    • If the target font is meant to be imported by a CDN like Google or Adobe as in the case of our current Lato, Roboto, and Inter fonts, follow these steps. As described above, navigate to your system-installed fonts. Ensure that the target font is NOT installed on your computer. Delete if necessary. Next, open Chrome Dev Tools (directly from the sign-in page of your testing environment) and begin tracking network requests (you may want to filter for Font requests only). We want to ensure that the browser doesn't have the font cached already, so clear your browser cache by right-clicking the refresh icon and clicking "Clear cache and hard reload". This forces your browser to connect to the CDN for the font. If you see that one or more font requests took a nonzero amount of time, that means it called the CDN. Now if you do a simple refresh, it will not call the CDN, so the request will still appear but it will have a time of 0ms since it got the font the first time. I'm aware that the font names are encrypted, but I think you just have to deal with it. So now since you know the import process happened, you can test for correctness by attempting to see the font displayed in the app.
    • If the target font is meant to be imported via a downloaded file, follow these steps. As described above, navigate to your system-installed fonts. Ensure that the target font is NOT installed on your computer. Delete if necessary. Next, as described above, clear your browser's cache (with or without hard reload on your testing environment is fine). Then simply navigate to a view which you expect to see the target font. If you see it at all, then the import worked because there is no other way for your browser to display the correct font.

@Jennifer Hickey @Robert Hobbs @Walter Martinez 

This comment will serve as a summary of the output we had from the meeting on 2/10/25:

  • developer will comment on the discussion we had about multiple attributes for a single font variable as opposed to specifying multiple attributes separately
  • product owner will give more thought to the subject of the first bullet point of the AC; also, we will consider in the future supporting multiple fonts; however, for now, we will not be making any code changes; thus, no QA responsibilities necessary
  • developer will document as many web-safe fonts acceptable to use in VDS as possible
  • developer will document guidance on making use of downloaded fonts
  • developer will document if and how it is possible to know whether browser will make use of already available font or if fetching of any kind is necessary (this will aid in future testing efforts to ensure that a fetch was actually performed)

@Robert Hobbs @Reid Wilson @Walter Martinez
Documenting - question posed on how a new font can be added to the site without deployment.

@Daniel Arwe @Robert Hobbs @Walter Martinez All text looks good.

@Robert Hobbs, @Jennifer Hickey, @Walter Martinez  In the spirit of full disclosure, here are some screenshots to inform the QA process (as promised in our demo. this morning, QA folks).

Overlay Frames (in order of appearance):



Here is a screenshot of the indicator that tells the user whether or not the inspector is on....
@Robert Hobbs @Jennifer Hickey @Walter Martinez @Charlie Bradley III  Pasting here for visibility....

@Robert Hobbs @Jennifer Hickey @Walter Martinez @Charlie Bradley III continuing the discussion started with Rob and Jennifer below....

I think I'll go with something a bit nicer than a JS alert box.  Even though this is an internal utility that only BOT users will see, I don't want them getting the impression that it is "cheap" or quickly thrown together, since that will likely color their perception of the tool going forward and its usefulness.

This is what I propose instead...

  1.  When the user presses CTRL-SPACE to activate, an overlay appears that tells the user the following...
    1. That they've turned the tool on
    2. How to use the tool and what information they can expect to see, and in what format (so they know how to interpret the information provided)
    3. How to turn it off
  2. The user dismisses the overlay with a button that reads "OK", "Got it", "I understand", or something else.
  3. When the user dismisses the overlay, while the tool is active/turned on, a small indicator is displayed at the top (see green box on screenshot below for location) that says "Theme Variable Helper ON", below that (smaller text) it has "CTRL-SPACE to turn OFF" and has an 'i' icon (I was thinking I would just reuse the one from the visualizer in DMH).  When the 'i' icon is clicked the overlay is displayed again, so the user can reference the full instructions again, if desired.
  4. When the user presses CTRL-SPACE to deactivate, the indicator goes away.
  5. When the user activates the tool, the code checks for a local storage key, indicating if the overlay needs to be displayed on activation.  This would prevent the overlay from appearing automatically every time the user turns the tool on.  If a user has seen the overlay and used the tool before, they don't need the instructions each time and they can always refer back to them by clicking the 'i' to view them again.  If its a user's first time, the value would not be set, and they would see the overlay with instructions.
I can send additional text and screenshots, as I build out the instructions and overlay content, assuming this idea is kosher with everyone.

Please chime in with feedback, thoughts, and suggestions.  Thanks!

@Jennifer Hickey @Daniel Arwe No strong feelings about it.

"Theme Helper activated - hover over elements to inspect variables.
To turn this feature off, press CTRL+SPACE."

@Daniel Arwe @Walter Martinez @Robert Hobbs @Joe Ebeling 
I'm concerned about our ability to meet our sprint commitment and wanted to outline my thoughts.

This story has scope creep. We tend to only think of scope creep as something developers have to manage to prevent their development efforts from being derailed, but scope creep is an unexpected and added burden to QA's function as well. Scope creep impacts development (coding and testing) time needed for a story and introduces risk in a story not finishing primary development. We fail to recongize that scope creep, weather requested by QA, PO or dev, also impacts the testing effort. These additional efforts are not accounted for in the original score. Just as developer should protect their ability to complete user stories, QA should also ensure the team's sprint commitment can be met and evaluate our ability to accommodate scope creep within a sprint. There is no question that we all want to deliver value in a timely fashion. Scope creep happens and can be valuable when accommodated for. My goal here is to bring our attention to the collective impacts of scope creep on the whole team.

In this user story, the added Shortcuts functionality does not have AC written for it. This story is In Dev which means QA is currently writing test cases along side the developer writing code. QA will become blocked in our "In Dev" work until the developer can determine and write AC for this functionality. (Some ex of AC needed: max number of shortcut variables allowed, min and max char length of name, is name required, is name unique, will the name be trimmed, how does a user delete a shortcut variable, edit a shortcut variable, what is expected when a user clicks the "add" button, where/how will these be stored in the db. @Daniel Arwe can add these as they are decided upon/implemented). QA did ask many questions at prestory meeting when made aware of the new functionality, but not expecting the additional scope, we likely did not ask all questions needed and will have to revisit unknowns. 

This story is already at risk for completion as 8's often take the whole sprint to develop. Testing will take longer than originally planned with the new functionality and more functionality increases the risk for QA returning stories to dev. It should be expected that scope creep on an 8 exaserbates this risk. I am by no means saying the scope creep is not needed or desired here. The added functionality certainly adds value, but I do want the team to be aware if the implecations and risk.

@Daniel Arwe @Walter Martinez @Joe Ebeling
Implementation decisions QA will need to know prior to PR:
  • screenshots/demo of UI for "pre-test"
  • user workflow to enter edit mode, make edits, exit edit mode?
  • What text, if any, will appear in video tab to alert user it is still being developed
  • In text editor, what is max char length? What happens if exceeded?
  • mechanism that will show the default variable is being used
  • mechanism to differentiate the default, edited default, theme specific

Developer Collab. Notes:

@Reid Wilson, @Justin Pope.....My early thoughts on the backend design/architecture.  Haven't worked out every little detail, obviously, but this is a good start.  Open to feedback. Thanks!

Backend Design

  1. Endpoints:
    1. UpdateThemeVariableValues
      1. Takes theme lookup key and list of variable value update DTOs
      2. Returns a response object containing information about which variable values were updated and which were inserted
      3. All possible, non-theme-specific theme variables would have values, either
        1. Default theme values OR
        2. Theme specific values (where default values are overridden)
      4. For each variable value....
        1. If existing record, update
        2. If no existing record, insert
    2. GetThemeVariableValues
      1. Takes theme lookup key
      2. Returns a list of DTOs for the variable values, with a flag giving the fronted knowledge of which values are default values.
      3. The thinking here is to let the backend sort out which values reflect default theme values and which ones are being overridden by the specific custom theme.

Prestory meeting questions:

  1.  Any need for error messaging at this point, other than maybe "something went wrong and your stuff didn't save"?  I'm thinking we build in more robust messaging later, or better client-side validation to prevent most (if not all) possible user mistakes.

@Robert Hobbs @Victor Monterrey @Jennifer Hickey

Here are some screenshots demoing the new functionality with every case covered I can think of. You will see that:
  • small images will scale up a ton
  • horizontal-dominant images will still fill up the width but not fill up the height
  • it is still possible for a single page to house multiple plan images (they will probably have to be horizontal-dominant)

@Jennifer Hickey

Hi Jennifer, I can answer your questions now with details we discussed in the pre-story meeting.

No image will appear small after this story regardless of its internal size. We will be scaling up the width of all images as much as possible. That means your question about whether or not they should be centered doesn't really apply because there will be no wiggle room on either side of the image. However, in the [edge, maybe?] case where we have to render a "very vertical" image (one where the height is much greater than the width) it is possible for the height restriction to prevent the image from growing further before it reaches the full width of the page. In that case, we decided that the image should be centered horizontally. One thing I am not changing is the max image height which is set to 9.4in or 12.4in for letter and legal modes respectively (recall those options in the selections report options menu). Also recall that because the header takes up space on the first page and because we are keeping the max image height constant, that means there could be a small amount of whitespace at the bottom of subsequent pages.

We also discussed the "configuration" part of the AC. Rob decided that these changes will go straight into the master branch with zero configuration. So that means no selections report option, no builder feature, etc.. His reasoning is that our users likely won't be uploading tiny images that wouldn't look very good, and if they complain about it, then maybe later we can do some kind of configuration.

21792.text: Dorliza has made changes which fix this.

This one is an interesting case. The text color for a selected option is given the "color-primary" variable while unselected options are hard-coded to a dark-ish gray color. So they are supposed to be different by design. For Historymaker, it must mean that this light gray color is set as the primary color. What I would recommend here is to not set gray as the primary color. Red should probably be the primary color. However, I understand I am not the theme designer here. If the desired solution is having a color to set for the text of selected options that affects nothing else, then a new variable would need to be created.

31631.text:
✅QA Validation Completed!

It is resolving for the duplicated plan.

🐛 Reproducing the issue. 

For this test, we created a duplicated plan inserted directly into the DB since we cannot create Plans, Tenant Plans or Profile Plans with duplicated names. 

Plan Under Test: TP01 


Homebuyer Under Test: 5A2E38E5-4F0B-4285-ADFA-B1623713E91A
Tenant Id Under Test: 67222232-b61d-48b4-9581-c9d25763f841

Profile Plan: 
Lakes of Pine Forest | NONE | Aransas 


In plans: 
Aransas 1234 
Aransas 5678 

Scenario: both plans exist as tenant plans 

Lakes of Pine Forest (ID) | NONE (ID) | Aransas 1234
Lakes of Pine Forest (ID) | NONE (ID) | Aransas 5678 
In this case, with 2 matching tenant plan combos, we can't know which one is "correct" 
In the case that they are registering a buyer using this tenant plan ID, and then contracting the buyer. It could FAIL to properly create a session. This because the data in VCMS may be using the integration ID for Aransas 5678 and not 1234 

Scenario: only 1 exists as a tenant plan 
Lakes of Pine Forest (ID) | NONE (ID) | Aransas 5678
Call succeeds. The session creation for this scenario SHOULD work properly only because the VCMS data should not be configured for a plan where tenant plan combinations don't exist. 

The only way to ensure this doesn't happen downstream is to prevent duplicate plans.

@Robert Hobbs @Justin Pope @Jennifer Hickey @Walter Martinez @Victor Monterrey

Here are the highlights of the pre-story meeting.

Justin and I informed the team that we feel it is necessary to convert the JavaScript files running every other dashboard to TypeScript. That means we would like the tester to do a brief "pulse check" of all other dashboard types, namely homebuyer dashboard, designer dashboard, sales counselor dashboard, public dashboard (sign-in page), and blank dashboard (for sys-admins). What does "pulse check" mean? We mean that we do not want you spending your time doing an extensive regression test of everything that the dashboard does. If the dashboard loads up, looks fine, and maybe a button click or two does what is expected, then we think it passes the pulse check. We think it's unlikely for any minute thing to be broken; it is more likely that the whole dashboard works like it previously did or not at all.

We do want the tester to extensively test the new dashboard we are creating for this new role. That means not only loading it up but also clicking every button on the screen and anything else that QA typically does to try to break the app. We will be providing a screenshot of what it should look like as soon as Rob answers our below question regarding the Register Contract shortcut (and also I may need to return from PTO).

Rob decided that this new legacy role will be called "Sales Counselor (Scheduling Only)". So anytime you see a dropdown detailing all legacy roles, this one should be included as well and that is exactly how its name should be spelled. Rob also said that the sales counselor dropdown field on profile plans should be modified to include users of this new role type. So instead of just populating with users of role sales counselor, users of this new role type will be included as well. Finally, Rob says we should grant user of this new role type access to the user registration page. They should only be able to register users of role homebuyer. This is exactly how we treat sales counselors when they try to register a new user.

Please get in touch with Justin or me if you have any questions. Thanks.

@Robert Hobbs

Justin and I have a draft for the new role's dashboard. The shortcuts module right now is exactly the same as for sales counselors. However, we were concerned about the Register Contract shortcut. Should that shortcut/menu item/page be accessible for users of this role?

22564.text:
Product Owner Summary (future use): 
  • a postman collection is attached to the work item with end points for posting errors to both logs. 
    • Check the collection variables for your environment and user auth 
    • the vds api requires an email and password and run the authenticate vds post first to get a bearer token 
    • the integration api requires an api key which can be found in VEO admin for the environment you are using under Integration Clients -> VDS 
  • messages are logged on the server bld-vdswebdev01 in websites and for [environment].veodesignstudio.com there is a logs_VDS folder and a log file (general logs) and unhandled_exceptions (thrown exceptions log).

@<6E668580-F8AB-64C3-A4C1-1DDE45503DA2> , @<B812E019-216E-6901-A7EC-6A2CF899BC54> , @<16695B85-2F2C-6860-B106-056F5D5396E1> , @<4C46EFE8-26F9-6F48-B257-971123DDB984> This is likely out of scope for this story, but perhaps fodder for another story.  When deploying to DEV environment and testing, I noticed we hit the /health endpoint a lot, and this will likely clutter the log and make it less useful to the Integration Team.  In a future story, we may want to filter out some requests from getting logged, perhaps based on endpoint signature....

![image.png](https://dev.azure.com/BuildOnTechnologies/ddde192e-e31e-4e51-af4b-3b7b4221266a/_apis/wit/attachments/162a98d0-0800-4ddf-8343-9d4238ebb78f?fileName=image.png)

@<16695B85-2F2C-6860-B106-056F5D5396E1> , @<4C46EFE8-26F9-6F48-B257-971123DDB984> 
For testing purposes, the URLs for the **endpoints to trigger an exception and log a message**, via Postman are as follows...

**VDS:
{BaseUrl}/api/logging/log/{level}
{BaseUrl}/api/exception/throw

Integration API:
{BaseApiUrl}/logging/log/{level}
{BaseApiUrl}/exception/throw**

...where **BaseUrl** and **BaseApiUrl** are defined in the **environment library in ADO**...

![image.png](https://dev.azure.com/BuildOnTechnologies/ddde192e-e31e-4e51-af4b-3b7b4221266a/_apis/wit/attachments/ece1221c-c6b2-461e-930a-a195391c0a42?fileName=image.png) 

...and **level** is the **level of the log message**.  **Reminder:  the NLog.config is configured to only log messages of level 'error' or above, so hitting the endpoint with a level of 'information' or 'warning' will NOT result in a log entry being recorded.  Only hitting the endpoint with a level of 'error' will cause a log entry to be recorded.  The request to the exception/throw endpoint will always result in a log entry in the unhandled-exceptions log file.**

These two files should be visible, provided at least 1 log message and 1 exception have been trigger, at the path **'C:\logs_VDS' and 'C:\logs_IntegrationAPI' for VDS and the Integration API respectively.**

![image.png](https://dev.azure.com/BuildOnTechnologies/ddde192e-e31e-4e51-af4b-3b7b4221266a/_apis/wit/attachments/0e7e4dc2-ee7b-4e90-ae3f-b07f653599dd?fileName=image.png)

@<4C46EFE8-26F9-6F48-B257-971123DDB984> @<16695B85-2F2C-6860-B106-056F5D5396E1>  When testing and using the endpoints for logging a message of a specific level, triggering a message of level 'warning' or 'information' will not cause a log entry to be sent to the log file.  This is because the current NLog.config file defines a rule that says to only log messages of level Error or above....FYI

![image.png](https://dev.azure.com/BuildOnTechnologies/ddde192e-e31e-4e51-af4b-3b7b4221266a/_apis/wit/attachments/4816b7d8-fb7b-4540-adb8-abd8d6ad1276?fileName=image.png)

Since we need a way for QA to trigger throwing an exception or logging a message (to verify they are logged to the correct target file), without the ability to change the code to do so, I am going to add a 'ThrowException' and a 'LogMessageOfLevel' endpoint for VDS API and Integration API to allow for this.  Adding tasks in the board for this. @<16695B85-2F2C-6860-B106-056F5D5396E1> @<4C46EFE8-26F9-6F48-B257-971123DDB984> @<6E668580-F8AB-64C3-A4C1-1DDE45503DA2> @<B812E019-216E-6901-A7EC-6A2CF899BC54>

@<4C46EFE8-26F9-6F48-B257-971123DDB984> @<16695B85-2F2C-6860-B106-056F5D5396E1> @<B812E019-216E-6901-A7EC-6A2CF899BC54> @<6E668580-F8AB-64C3-A4C1-1DDE45503DA2> Adding additional KVP called '**EnableRequestLogging_IntegrationAPI**' for toggling request logging on/off as described in the AC.

@<4C46EFE8-26F9-6F48-B257-971123DDB984> , on the server, this can be found at **C:\Websites\\{integrationApiUrl}\appsettings.json**, in order to toggle this for testing purposes.

@<16695B85-2F2C-6860-B106-056F5D5396E1> @<4C46EFE8-26F9-6F48-B257-971123DDB984> @<6E668580-F8AB-64C3-A4C1-1DDE45503DA2> Another slight change for production environments....
![image.png](https://dev.azure.com/BuildOnTechnologies/ddde192e-e31e-4e51-af4b-3b7b4221266a/_apis/wit/attachments/5638cb0c-f204-4623-8438-2fcab1f3ca92?fileName=image.png)

@<16695B85-2F2C-6860-B106-056F5D5396E1> , @<6E668580-F8AB-64C3-A4C1-1DDE45503DA2> , @<4C46EFE8-26F9-6F48-B257-971123DDB984> Please note the following slight change to the info. below....

@<16695B85-2F2C-6860-B106-056F5D5396E1> @<B812E019-216E-6901-A7EC-6A2CF899BC54> @<4C46EFE8-26F9-6F48-B257-971123DDB984> @<6E668580-F8AB-64C3-A4C1-1DDE45503DA2>

![image.png](https://dev.azure.com/BuildOnTechnologies/ddde192e-e31e-4e51-af4b-3b7b4221266a/_apis/wit/attachments/a8ee79aa-c9e6-44a2-af19-08f3a36067dd?fileName=image.png) 
![image.png](https://dev.azure.com/BuildOnTechnologies/ddde192e-e31e-4e51-af4b-3b7b4221266a/_apis/wit/attachments/a0f9bf24-de69-4c19-b909-36b56655b0bc?fileName=image.png) 

In order to create a separate location for 'log' and 'unhandled-exceptions', exclusively for the Integration API logs to get written to, I am going to create a new key called '**NLogFilePath_IntegrationAPI**', with values in a format similar to the above, of '**C:\VDS_IntegrationAPI Logs\\{stack}**', for the 4 production stacks.  **For all lower environments, since the current value is just 'logs', I'll use 'logs_IntegrationAPI' to differentiate.**

In the NLog.transform.config file (shown below), the value of 'logDirectory' gets replaced with the value corresponding to the appropriate key, described above.  Currently, since both NLog.transform.config files (VDS and Integration API) reference the same key in appsettings (corresponding with the KVPs set in the Library in ADO for each stack) logs from both VDS and Integration API (for both normal logs and unhandled exceptions) get written to the same log files.

![image.png](https://dev.azure.com/BuildOnTechnologies/ddde192e-e31e-4e51-af4b-3b7b4221266a/_apis/wit/attachments/ef80f337-cb6d-4fc1-8ed8-be1579dde7e7?fileName=image.png) 

So, in order to point the Integration API to log to its own log files, in its NLog.transform.config file, we will instead point the logDirectory variable to use the value of the **NLogFilePath_IntegrationAPI** key.

@Reid Wilson @Robert Hobbs 

After looking at the stored procedure that fetches the photos to be displayed, the following (which Chat helped with) lays out the conditions that detemine whether or not a photo_id is returned for a given photo, for display in the Installation Photos module:


Boiling the logic down to specifically photo_attributes and photo_attributes_values, and discounting the obvious requirements for things like valid security token and valid customer, the 'Part Number' and 'Area' attributes need to have values.  The Part Number attribute value is used to determine, via the colors and styles_groups_detail tables, respectively, whether or not a part is valid and whether or not it is part of a customer's program.  The Area atribute value is used in 2 ways.  First of all, it is used to determine the area label to display for the photo in the UI.  More importantly, if an area filter value is passed (to include only certain areas) the Area attribute is used to determine inclusion/exclusion in the returned photo_id list.

As to Dorliza's second question about photos being "global," or visible to all builders, in the current system this is not possible, since the stored procedure that grabs the photo_id list for images to display takes account_id and organization_id parameters used to determine the customer from the customers table.  In my estimation, that stored procedure would have to be modified to accept NULL or empty values for these parameters and logic to handle those cases (along with changes to support such a scenario futher upstream--use case, endpoint, frontend service, etc.).

I can pass this information along to Dorliza and see if it answers all her questions satisfactorily, however, I did have a question, @Robert Hobbs for you first....

Is making images available "globally", for all builders, something we want to support in VDS?  I can't think of a scenario where that would be beneficial, but maybe there's something I'm not considering.  I didn't want to present the information about doing that to Dorliza if it's not something we even want VDS to be able to do.

@Daniel Arwe @Robert Hobbs

Here is the relevant part of the message I received from Dorliza earlier today. It may be helpful. I am telling her to reach out to Daniel for future comments about this story.

"We are trying to figure out what are the parameters for installation photos that allow it to be shown in VDS, ie what infomation is required, and is there a way to make an install photo "global" or visible to all builders? Rob says that's never been documented what attributes are needed"

@Walter Martinez @Jennifer Hickey 

I found that the issue occurs only with the font which Historymaker uses, "proxima-nova", and when the screen resolution approaches laptop size or smaller such that a second line is necessary to display the whole sentence. A hard-coded CSS change was made in this area which fixes the issue. The change, like the DMH nav menu change, is simply to apply a line-height of 1.2 to the text. This will cause there to be less whitespace between lines for the Proxima Nova font. Now you will see that the text still comes almost too close to the dot elements, but there is not much I can do about that since there is no real estate left in the card. But I would say the change is a definite improvement, and that the elements no longer overlap each other. No other areas in the site should be affected. All other themes should appear unaffected.
