While I was unable to reproduce the "flat rate" scenario described above in the lower environments, I was able to do so in the WBS production stack.  Forcing a reprice did not change this.  I still don't know the "why" behind this, but at least we can see where it is happening.  If we need to dig deeper into determining a cause, we may need to point local development environment at production and set some breakpoints.  I am back burner-ing this at the moment, per @Robert Hobbs, in favor of moving forward on discussions with the Echelon team (preliminary phase is a review of the associated wiki for this story by @Roger Wang) as far as what Echelon needs to implement a change that moves the repricing logic to the Echelon side of the fence.

Session ID: 33fce680-1cf4-4397-8568-f4f54c0b8850

@Robert Hobbs Here is a wiki I created documenting my findings:

https://dev.azure.com/BuildOnTechnologies/VeoDesignStudio/_wiki/wikis/VeoDesignStudio/2635/18399-Research-BOM-Pricing-Repricing-Logic-Documentation

@Daniel Arwe hey Daniel, when we talk about snapshot we are talking about all the tables that comprise the session object. Reprice is a generic term that happens in a few ways: 
  • an area is fully selected in design selections and there is a "reprice" (looks like refresh button) 
  • when a tile pattern is selected and the bom changes the pricing is reworked 
  • when refresh session is run and aspects of the session snapshot are updated with new data from Echelon (or veo) 
In terms of tracking down all the tables involved, there is a stored proc that creates a session that may be useful as a starting point.  Unfortunately the tables are rather badly named so it's difficult to intuit from SQL alone  

For the full picture, I would get with Roger first, then Shelby next.

@Robert Hobbs, when we talk about the snapshot in the AC, are we talking about the data in the VEO database that is copied from WBS_Staging, via DataSync?  I didn't think so, but if this is not the case, when we snapshot Echelon data for the purpose of determining prices as the designer makes selections, where does that snapshot get stored?

Also, when we talk about a BOM lines getting repriced, is the act of repricing essentially saying, "Don't use the snapshotted price for these BOM lines, but instead, go and fetch the latest live pricing data from Echelon."?

28970.text:
@<6E668580-F8AB-64C3-A4C1-1DDE45503DA2> **Here are the relevant details you requested on the cause of the issue.  Long story short, there was a plan plan in Veo_plan_mstr DB table that was not marked address specific and thus was getting filtered out of the list returned to populate the dropdown.  This caused the first plan, rather than the correct plan, to be selected in the dropdown and, when the SAVE action was executed the backend saw it as a plan change (due to the plan name on the profile plan saved and the plan in the Veo_plan_mstr table not matching) and kicked it back due to the validation checking whether or not there were sessions on the profile plan.  The same plan in Echelon WAS marked address specific, so the issue was data-related and once the value was set correctly in Veo_plan_mstr, the issue was resolved.  No code changes were required.**

![==image_2==.jpeg](https://dev.azure.com/BuildOnTechnologies/ddde192e-e31e-4e51-af4b-3b7b4221266a/_apis/wit/attachments/7bd6f859-2af8-43dc-81c9-a8c2befbdf97?fileName===image_2==.jpeg) 

If the Sales Counselor switches to user impersonation, the logic for selecting the C > S > P, from the Profile Plans for the impersonated user, is as follows:
*   Find the first CONTRACTED plan....return if found
*   Find the first COMPLETED plan...return if found
*   Find the first PROSPECTIVE  or INACTIVE plan (based on feature flag)...return if found
*   If none of the above found, return undefined
The modOptionPricing dashboard module then determines what to display based on what is returned.
*   hasActivePlan() => User impersonation control (label showing C > S > P for active plan and "Launch Option Pricing" button
*   !hasActivePlan() => Sales Counselor control (C > S > P dropdowns)
So, filtering for Sales Counselor Dashboard will involve...
*   Filtering Community dropdown (non-impersonation view) for only Communities assigned to the SC user.*   We can likely do this here, when communities that populate the dropdown are loaded:
![==image_0==.jpeg](https://dev.azure.com/BuildOnTechnologies/ddde192e-e31e-4e51-af4b-3b7b4221266a/_apis/wit/attachments/33f0bc36-3d00-4124-bb3a-5b6ec03bc72f?fileName===image_0==.jpeg) 
*   Filtering which plans to retrieve, from impersonated user's Profile Plans, to only include C > S > P combinations where Community is in the Assigned Communities list for the SC user.

Given the color input / color picker:
What to do when first entering the color picker:
1- nothing:
  pro: easy to code
  con: not such a great UX
  - we can give a default color of white that could be a little bit better of a default
2 - give the color input initial value from text input
   Issue 1: color input only reads in hexcodes
      Solutions: parse / conversion logic to know what we can give the color input
          Parse/Converstion:
             Need to see HEX, Color Words, RGB, and HSL (not a binding list)
             If these types, convert to HEX code and give to color picker
          If parse failed:
            - color picker defaults to black or a determined default

@[VeoDesignStudio]\VeoDesignStudio Team

After Action Report

Operation: Support & Success Collaboration
Date/Time: 2025-09-11, 1300–1500
Personnel Involved: Dorliza, Chloe (S&S Content Team)

1. Mission

Engage with S&S Content Team personnel to identify, validate, and resolve issues in the handling of catalog items and package submissions.

2. Execution

Coordination was conducted directly with Dorliza and Chloe. The effort focused on validating data entries, reviewing package submissions, and confirming configuration accuracy. End-to-end testing was performed, including rendering submissions with Aareas and verifying room visualization.

3. Observations

The following issues were identified during the session:

Catalog items were not consistently entered.

Packages were submitted without corresponding Aareas.

Multiple package configurations contained errors, including:

Carpet incorrectly assigned as countertop.

Surface configurations misaligned with requirements.

4. Issues Identified

Lack of proper data validation at initial entry.

Gaps in submission verification prior to acceptance.

Configuration issues revealed limited knowledge of self-validation processes.

5. Corrective Actions Executed

Communicated identified issues to S&S Content Team.

Oversaw resubmittal of packages with correct configurations.

Confirmed that Content Team does not directly manage catalog items; responsibility was escalated to proper channels.

Provided Dorliza with methods for self-validating data points.

Noted assumption that much of data validation responsibility is handled by Aaron.

use email and password for login in okta. Go to url: https://integrator-5980701.okta.com/ to see their dashboard.

I have modified OIDC configuration and domain mapping to set the 'dev okta' instance to map to the domain okta.com.

@[VeoDesignStudio]\VeoDesignStudio Team

Discovery of this happen while tinkering around with our DEV setup of okta.
This is a standaridized approach for OIDC.

A brief look into this is that modern web apps do not use 3rd party cookies anymore.
What we would want to do is have a revamped "Remember Me" feature. We can store Authentication requests on the server and have a token stored on the client that matches to the authentication request. Upon revisting, the app can see the token and match to the authentication, at that point it is just a matter of refreshing and get into the app.

Service and Support has issues because they login to different users
We can account for this to not automatically sign them in but give them an option to sign in as that user, or let them login as a different user. With this we can stash different authenticated users as cookies and give them options of what users to login as.

Another Thought: Switch loggedin user
When logged in, have a drop down menu under the user to show other users to sign in as.

@Reid Wilson @Robert Hobbs @Walter Martinez Looks good to me. Big differance.

We lose a touch of the "extra fancy" that DMH offers, but readibility is more important.

@Jennifer Hickey @Robert Hobbs 

This screenshot shows the Design Selections nav menu without its text-shadow property. Does this look acceptable for Historymaker theme? Note that the text-shadow was previously applied to both the prices and the application texts. Note also that Option Pricing does not use any text shadow, so this is in line with Option Pricing.
@Reid Wilson @Robert Hobbs What is the plan for the item right below this one in the discussion. Is the red with shadow on grey going to stay? I don't see a note about it and it hasn't been changed.

@Reid Wilson @Robert Hobbs @Walter Martinez DS wasn't part of this originally. I noticed this in DS (The color change from dark grey background to light grey made this new issue). The red with a shadow is super hard to read.

@Robert Hobbs @Reid Wilson @Walter Martinez The red in the spending summary really should be white. Red on that grey kinda "glows" because our brains can't differentiate areas so close on the color wheel. I promise I'm not making this up. They are called analogous colors and they can be used to create a glow effect - like purple and black on web designs. But when we try to read the "glowing" text it is hard. (Feeling like I should explain my knowledge here, I have an undergrad degree which required many design classes.)
@Robert Hobbs @Reid Wilson @Walter Martinez  works for me.

@Reid Wilson @Jennifer Hickey @Walter Martinez So my eyes are definitely not young. I will share my opinion. It's an improvement, but the red is still difficult to read, especially on that dark gray in the selections summary panel. That red as a font color only really looks good on a light background (white or very light gray which is on their website) as in here: 



 In other places they use black with white text or red with white text as in here: 



They also just use black text in many places, so I think that's still an option for lighter areas. 

Similarly, the white font is harder to read on that lighter gray panel for option pricing nav. In that case you really want it darker if you are going with a white font. 

So, it's an improvement and if we are out of time I suppose we roll with it. If we have time I would suggest to go either lighter and use the red font or darker and use white (or red with white font). 

That seems most compatible with their website anyway.

@Jennifer Hickey @Walter Martinez @Robert Hobbs

Ok how about this version? Is it just my young eyes or is this decent enough? I acknowledge it still may not be readable enough so just let me know if that is the case. I changed the red of a variable called "text-primary-alt1" which covers all red prices on this screen. I changed it to a slightly brighter red which I grabbed from Historymaker's website, so it is canon, lol. If it is acceptable, I will provide documentation for all places in the app affected by "text-primary-alt1" but I wanted to get approval on this screenshot before I embark on that.

@Jennifer Hickey @Walter Martinez @Charlie Bradley III

After consulting with Charlie, we agreed on colors to use for the background, application text, and price text for the left panel. That has been implemented in the screenshot below. However, there are more places in the app where prices show in red for Historymaker theme, even right there directly in Option Pricing. QA has recently said that prices should not show in red, but it was unclear the extent of that statement. With the updated left panel, I am wondering if many other locations need to be updated. Surrounded in a blue square in the screenshot are places in Option Pricing which traditionally would use the primary color (which looks great for pretty much all themes, and it happens to be red for Historymaker). My question is, is this screenshot good as is? Or do we need to change these other locations in Option Pricing as well? Or do we need to go through the whole site and make sure that no price appears in red ever? I had to make a new variable called "text-price" to accomplish the black prices you see on the left panel. I cannot simply apply "text-price" to the prices on the right panel because the black on the dark panel will not be read-able enough. The scope of this is increasing drastically and I want to be sure I get QA's stance before I do any potentially unnecessary work.

@Jennifer Hickey @Robert Hobbs @Walter Martinez @Daniel Arwe

There are 3 sub-issues that make up this task. I developed the fix for the text on what I call the central buttons (Select Products and Selection Report) and also the action buttons (the 6 buttons that sometimes appear on the right side of the session card and sometimes at the bottom of the session card, depending on screen resolution). Daniel developed the fix for the Report Visibility area, but I know how he did it, so I can explain that here too.

1) For the 2 central buttons, I applied the font size maximizer to it. That means the font size will be set to the largest possible without doing anything weird like overflowing or going onto a second line.

2) For the 6 action buttons, I applied the font size maximizer to it. That means the font size will be set to the largest possible without doing anything weird like overflowing or going onto a second line.

3) For the report visibility area, Daniel told me that he changed some CSS properties to make it look good. The font size will be static (not dynamic).

Please reach out with any questions about this.

@Robert Hobbs @Reid Wilson @Walter Martinez I would prefer testing outside fonts and all the variability that goes with that to be incorporated within a story that ensures we handle fonts across the system. I was concerned there was no future story to do that.

If there is, then I'm comfortable testing the "supported" fonts for now using Montserrat as the large font as recommended by Reid.

Hopefully this was what you were  asking me to comment on... 

@Reid Wilson  So is the fix for this dynamic or manual in nature? is it automatically resizing the font to try to fit the text (meaning a really wide font just gets smaller) ? or is it something else? 

@Jennifer Hickey @Walter Martinez
My initial opinion (not dictating anything regarding the testing strat) is to validate the fonts we know about now and defer the future proofing just a bit with a new work item to examine fonts for *potentially* upcoming themes. 

I say this because 
  • I don't have a current ordered list of upcoming themes content will be doing and so there may be a delay there to assemble one 
  • we may want to improve how we get new fonts into the theming system which might lower the cost of testing somewhat (oh to dream!) 
So just my op, let us know what you think is best based on that.

@Reid Wilson @Walter Martinez @Robert Hobbs 
I need feedback on this statement - Any font not in the list is not officially supported and should not be guaranteed to work. 

While I understand that these are teh fonts we support at this minute, I was also under the impression, that each tiem S&S needs a new font for a builder, we will add it to the site. That makes the idea of officially supported fonts null and we'll end up having to do extensive retesting every time a new theme has a new font. If we prefer to defer this effort to future stories, QA needs to know.

Typically QA would ask what the upcoming 10-20 new themes, look at those builder's fonts and the fonts of current builders via their websites and determine a resonable biggest font and smallest font that would be sutable for testing. That of course, woudl require a dev to add it if its not in teh current list for testing purposes.

@Walter Martinez @Jennifer Hickey

I recommend testing the eventual fix for this issue with the narrowest supported font and the widest supported font. Of course, QA may decide differently on how to test this issue, and that is fine; just let me know. You can pick from the following list of supported fonts. Any font not in the list is not officially supported and should not be guaranteed to work. That is our official policy as far as I am aware. I believe I have already sent Walter this list and put it in an earlier story, but I am writing it again here for reference. Justin had asked what font I currently have been working with while writing code, and I answered Montserrat, which is true. However, I was dictating nothing, so please let me know how QA will test this issue, and I will run through an identical test before handing over to QA.

Supported fonts:
    Web-safe fonts:
  • Arial
  • Verdana
  • Tahoma
  • Trebuchet MS
  • Times New Roman
  • Georgia
  • Garamond
  • Courier New
  • Brush Script MT
    Non-web-safe fonts but still supported:
  • Lato
  • Roboto
  • FigTree
  • Inter
  • Proxima Nova (proxima-nova)
  • Montserrat

@Robert Hobbs Totally agree. If the goal is that there isn't dev work associated with every new theme, we have to fix it sooner rather than later. We have no idea what fonts are coming down the pipeline. Of course, to even add a new font at this point requires dev work so we'll already have dev work for every new theme to get the font hardcoded.

@Jennifer Hickey the font problem is potentially breaking across all pages. My thought is that we either a) make adaptive components either using Reid's latest font maximizer (?) or through css means or b) we budget for all the headaches associated with a new font. Essentially a new font right now = another full story of changes with QA from what I can see.

@Reid Wilson I added a 3rd location - Structural Options text is being truncated.

✅QA validation completed! 

Executed manual happy path and also performed a new session creation and interacted with the OP menu. All working as expected.



SEE ATTACHED VIDEO FOR SESSION CREATION

Similar issues of time out and slow reponse time was experienced in the Intergration API as well as for viewing Option Pricing in production. The fix that was implemented was refining the way EF Core was building the query.

Points of noticed bottle necks were:

31544.text:
✅Jumpy images bug is also fixed. 😉 
Back to you, @Robert Hobbs

Technical Implementation to address this issue:

1) Backend retry and Binary Checking
The way we interacted with the GPC endpoint before was that we requested for the image and read the binary as is. The issue here is that if the request returned an error or the response was not an image, we were not checking for this.
Now, we validate that the image data is not empty or null and check that the content type is an image type. Also, we implemented retry logic to get the image again if it doesn't return a valid image.

2) Frontend ImageLoader potentially had stale bindings
Our Custom Image Loader bindings were not managing the bindings appropriately and potentially leaving around stale bindings. This could cause a false error that would trigger fetching the stacks missing product image.
We implemented better logic to handle managing these bindings so that stale bindings are not left hanging around.

📢 UNABLE TO REPRODUCE IN LOWER ENVIRONMENTS. 

QA NOTE
 
The bug was reported and confirmed in production. Attempts to reproduce it in DEV/QA/PREV/Staging using the documented STR were unsuccessful. The issue occurs intermittently in production only. A fix was applied and will roll all the way to Production. The fix applied is not breaking anything on its way to Production so, there was a short regression testing on the area, plus confirmation testing that can be done in Prod by the end user when the fix goes live will satisfy the exit criteria of this bug and therefore can be marked as Done. 

Steps to reproduce in production. 
  1. Log in as an Admin or Impersonate a Contracted Homebuyer.
  2. Navigate to Option Pricing. 
  3. Click to any 'Category' / 'Product' and switch from one to another repeatedly.
    1. This has to be quick. 
    2. You may switch at random.
    3. When you are doing this, try to map which Categories / Products does have an image. 
    4. This issue only impacts non-estimated card items.
  4. At some point you will notice that Products that had an image before now display the ‘stack-level missing image’ image and bypasses the ‘organization-level missing image’ image.
  5. Opening that product will show the correct image (but not in the thumbnail) 

@Robert Hobbs

@Robert Hobbs @Jennifer Hickey Rob, should the story proceed through QA without being held up, or should it be held in QA until the rounding mechanisms you referenced are investigated?  Reason I ask is because I think we still have some unanswered questions on the visualizer vignettes story and I was thinking that would take priority over investigating the rounding methods...at least in the short term for this spint.  Thoughts?

@Daniel Arwe @Jennifer Hickey so...not to blow this up but I think I need a bit of research on this one given the weirdness for rounding rules. 

Jim claims that for catalog items where we adjust the quantity we implemented a rule where the total is based on the rounded unit price not the original decimal value. So for example if the price is 83.33 for an item, with ceiling rounding we show 84 for the price. Jim is saying that if this were a regular catalog item and they change the quantity to 4 then the amount we would show is $336 (4 x 84) not $334 (83.33 x 4-> rounded). 

However, if we were to apply this same rule here then we would need to carry that calculation for these bom mods not only from the card but into the area total and ultimately the session total. 

So my question is - is what Jim is saying about catalog item rounding correct and if so how are we (mechanically) carrying that calculated amount into the session total now?

21786.text:
1: Dorliza has fixed this I believe.

2: Yes, we apply the variable "color-primary" to both the "Details" and "Compare" text colors. I recommend changing the value of that variable from gray to red.

3: The highlighted tab's text color uses the primary color wile the non-highlighted tab's text color is locked in as black. If you don't want the highlighted tab's text drawing from primary color and instead want it to be uniquely set, that will require a new variable. However, I don't recommend that. I recommend setting the "color-primary" variable to something other than gray, like red.

4: The color of the text "Lifestyle Snapshot" is assigned the variable "text-primary". The issue stems from the fact that the theme designer is using gray as a primary color which in Reid's opinion is probably a bad move. I would recommend setting "text-primary" to a shade of red. No development recommended, but if you really wanted to give this piece of text its own unique color, that will cost 1 variable for development.

5: This is a different location but the same issue as one defined in its own separate task. See my comment there.

6: The "More Information" text color draws from the "color-ds-text-primary" variable while the icon next to it draws its color from "color-icon-primary". If it is a problem that these 2 have different colors, there are 2 possible solutions. One possible solution is to have the theme designer set these variables to the same color. Another solution is to have the developer create 1 new custom variable to apply to the color of both this icon and this text. It is my opinion that this is not a big deal and we should not go around creating a new variable for tiny annoyances. There are many other places in the app that have this similar situation and it would be hypocritical to not create new variables in all of those locations as well. But I can make whatever necessary happen.

From Teams @[VeoDesignStudio]\VeoDesignStudio Team

Okay, so what I have found is that there is a bug (or serveral bugs, depending on how you want to count it) within our userManager.ts file.

When considering a CommunityId/CommunityGuid off of a profile plan, it is not guaranteed to come back. So not all of the filtering methods off the userManager is null checking them before trying to evaluate them.

Locally I have made changes to the following methods:

  • filterProfilePlansByUserAssignedCommunities
  • setVisibilityOfProfilePlansBasedOnAssignedCommunities
  • filterCommunitiesByUserAssignedCommunities
  • filterTenantPlanTreeByUserAssignedCommunities

With this change in place and signing into Test Sales, the community drop down is working approapriately:

 

So, with this there are two things we can do:

1- hotfix / wait until next release, my gut is telling me that the risk factor is a bit too high to wait until next release

2- What is causing the null?, so for this particular user, there is a profile plan that is not getting communities mapped to it correct and is causing a null on the community guid check.

for the user and using the following query:

 

SQL

select 
pp.[profile_id],
pp.[community_name],
csp.[community_id],
pp.[series],
csp.[series_id],
pp.[plan_name],
csp.[plan_id]
from 
[EPLAN_VEOSolutions].[dbo].[account_organization_user_profile_plan] pp
left join (
 SELECT DISTINCT
  csp.community_id,
  c.name as community_name,
  csp.series_id,
  s.name as series_name,
  csp.plan_id,
  p.name as plan_name
 FROM
  [EPLAN_VEOSolutions].[dbo].[VeoSolutionsSecurity_account_org_community_series_plan] csp
  LEFT JOIN [EPLAN_VEOSolutions].[dbo].VeoSolutionsSecurity_account_organization_communities c WITH (NOLOCK) ON c.account_id = csp.account_id AND c.organization_id = csp.organization_id AND c.community_id = csp.community_id
  LEFT JOIN [EPLAN_VEOSolutions].[dbo].VeoSolutionsSecurity_account_organization_series s WITH (NOLOCK) ON s.account_id = csp.account_id AND s.organization_id = csp.organization_id AND s.series_id = csp.series_id
  LEFT JOIN [EPLAN_VEOSolutions].[dbo].VeoSolutionsSecurity_account_organization_plans p WITH (NOLOCK) ON p.account_id = csp.account_id AND p.organization_id = csp.organization_id AND p.plan_id = csp.plan_id
 WHERE
  csp.account_id = '776095fe-aab4-4027-a23e-65264959cde4'
  AND csp.organization_id = 'b27b97c5-95a1-4420-b9aa-4958276d4e92' ) as csp on csp.plan_name = pp.plan_name AND csp.community_name = pp.community_name AND csp.series_name = pp.series
where pp.[user_id] = 'ee1aaabd-6c65-48f9-8333-9f6eb3a064ed'

Looking at use case GetSessionApplications.cs, we evaluate the application products before returning categories. Application and products that do not match to a category creates a category.

Implemenation strategies:
1) GetSessionCategories.cs should evaluate calalog_selections and make sure there is a catagory for each selection and back fill it is missing. If this approach is selected, we should refactor other use cases to point to this one when building categories for Option Pricing and Design Selections.

2) We can keep GetSessionCategories.cs as is and each module (Option Pricing, Design My Home, Design Selections) has a wrapper that builds the categories in the way they need them. This would suggest that we do minor refactoring to Option Pricing and Design Selections (ex. GetSessionApplications.cs) to fetch categories if we need session categories.

23486.text:
✅QA Validation Completed! 
PFA a quick screenshare on how now the user is able to login if the permission does not exist.

Findings from Reid during development:

  • forgetting to add a new permission in general does not cause the sign-in process to fail; the process will fail only if the forgotten permission is used to guard any routes; for example, the permission which inspired this bug is called "Can Manage Designers"; that one guards the designer documents route which is why forgetting to put it in the DB caused the sign-in process to fail
  • final logic for checking if the logged-in user has permission A:
    • can permission A be found?
      • Yes...
        • is permission A enabled?
          • Yes...
            • check the user's permissions as usual; return that value
          • No...
            • return true
      • No...
        • return false

Staging

@Reid Wilson @Walter Martinez @Jennifer Hickey 

This stuff is a bit complicated so bear with me here. 

In terms of the scheduler, when moving an appointment the user cannot opt to change the builder. 

They can move the appointment to any valid Designer meaning: 
  • a designer they can see in the scheduler based on their own settings
  • a designer that has the buyer's builder associated with the appointment they are trying to move
  • a designer that doesn't have an existing appointment in that time slot where there would be an overlap. 
There may also be a check on working location - I didn't have time to dig into that. 

Additionally at one point when moving an appointment the system tried to keep it limited to the original designer but I don't think this is working correctly. You can see the override checkbox for this here: 




So the builder filter you mention if it is working correctly should just filter out designers (and buyer cards I suppose) that are not associated with that builder and that you also have in your profile. It really shouldn't affect your ability to move an appointment to a designer, since if that designer is not setup for the buyer's builder they would be grayed out like this here. The designer grayed out does not have STAGGERED-QA: 



If I filter to a builder that is NOT staggered-qa, then some of the designers drop out, but those that remain have at least the builder you filtered for. In this example, this Buyer 103 is scheduled with a designer that is both STAGGERED-QA and TAYLOR MORRISON-HOUSTON. When I choose TM as my filter, this designer remains, but some others drop out. 



I am not sure if this is affecting the reschedule but notice that the Buyer 103 card on the left also drops out bc I am filtering by TM Houston. I have this feeling the move failure may be related to that card not being there anymore, but just a hunch. 

So now when I go to move this appointment (with the filter on) I am only seeing Taylor Morrison Houston designers. The same designer I started with is the only one that also has STAGGERED-QA so they show available for the move here: 




That last designer Spec Designer Staggered name is confusing I apologize for that - I removed STAGGERED-QA for this designer just to verify they would show as not available for the move. 

So to my knowledge it should allow us to reschedule this Buyer 103 appointment even with the filter set to a different builder. 

There is one more other configuration setting. In Settings you will notice a checkbox that indicates Hide on Filter. 
If this is marked, the system should be hiding unavailable designers instead of showing them grayed out. I played with it and found out it does work when scheduling a new appointment but not when dragging to move and existing appointment. 




Anyway, let me know if this helps to answer your questions. In practice it may never come up which is why they've not reported the issue OR it may be so old they don't see the point.

@Walter Martinez @Jennifer Hickey @Robert Hobbs

Good news / bad news. Or maybe I will just say interesting news. After a good bit of effort, I was successful in reproducing the issue. Here is what I figured out.

The app does not let you move an appointment associated with Builder A if the selected organization of the logged in user is set to a builder other than A, call it B. The reason that a user can view an appointment associated with Builder A is that designers can work for multiple builders. If Designer A works for Builder B, he/she will show up in the list. But if Designer A also works for Builder A too, then the appointments that pertain to Builder A will appear as well. Attempting to move those appointments will result in a silent error, and the app state is broken. This means that a refresh of the page is required to ensure user operations are performed correctly again.

I will admit that I do not understand much about the intended functionality of the selected organization or the organization filter. As such, I cannot immediately "fix" the issue because I do not know what the fix should do. For example, should such appointments not be viewable or should there be a no-click indicator when the user hovers their mouse over that kind of appointment? I will be looking for feedback on how to fix the bug. Thanks.

@<6E668580-F8AB-64C3-A4C1-1DDE45503DA2> Cole responded that VDS is set up as a round robin load balancer, with no weighting (all servers are equal in 'weight').  Traefik's documenation is very light as to what it means by vanilla 'round robin', so I asked my friend ChatGPT.  It confirmed (as best as ChatGPT will ever confirm), that each individual request is evaluated and sent to the next node in the list.  No other information is used to direct that request.  So, two users access VDS at the same time will have their requests interspersed, and one by one they will be directed to the next node (from the previous request).
@Eric Hickey can we track down exactly how the load balancing is working so we know how to handle support cases?

30900.text:
@<98EBA60A-973F-4D8F-90D4-24A629DD533B> 
This is a write up about the procedure created for live estimated data. This procedure has been deployed to the dev environment, pending @<B812E019-216E-6901-A7EC-6A2CF899BC54> process of the pipe line configuration, if you want to play around with it.

I am attaching files that represent the procedures on echelons side for refrence. Other references should be in our code base. Also a deployment file for prod with the synonyms needed in the environment.


# Procedure Explanation: `dbo.vds_selNonSessionEstimatedProductSearchOptions`
 

## Purpose
This stored procedure returns estimated product-search results for a non-session scenario.
The output shape is meant to align with the session-based product-search proc so the application can consume a consistent result set: 
- `source_type`

- `name`

- `application`

- `product`

- `area`

- `sub_area`

- `price`

- `price_level`

- `part_no`

- `item_no`

- `gpc_id`

- `build_id`

  

## What Problem It Solves

  

Before this proc, the non-session estimated search path was effectively stitched together from several older Yukon-era routines plus application-side orchestration:

  

1. Resolve the builder/external organization, community, series, and plan names.

2. Find the active spec.

3. Find the correct `prices_landed` effective date.

4. Find the correct `plan_id`.

5. Call logic to determine the max/default build per application/product/area/sub-area/location.

6. Call logic to get estimated price levels.

7. Resolve each returned price level into the actual selectable parts/colors.

8. Apply builder overrides and final search filtering.

  

The older approach spread that work across routines such as:

  

- `vds_selAllEstimatedOptionPricingItemsForNonSession_Yukon`

- `vds_optionPricingMaxMinBuilds`

- `vds_selGroupLevelParts_Yukon`

  

It also depended on application-side orchestration in `BuildOnTechnologies.VDS.Legacy.Dal\Services\OptionPricingService.cs`.

  

In `GetEstimatedGroupsForNonSessionAsync`, the legacy path first called:

  

- `vds_selAccountOrganizationMatchingSpecCommunities`

- `vds_selAccountOrganizationMatchingSpecSeries`

- `vds_selUnmappedAccountOrganizationPlans`

  

Those three result sets were then handed into `GetEstimatedOptionPricingItemsForNonSessionAsync`, where the service looped in priority order through:

  

- each matching community

- each matching series

- each unmapped plan

  

It repeatedly called into the repository until one community/series/plan combination returned rows.

  

In other words, the legacy non-session path used both multiple stored procedure calls up front and nested application loops afterward to arrive at the same candidate spec/plan combination that this new proc now resolves inline.

  

That made the flow harder to follow, more chatty, and more procedural than necessary.

  

## How the New Proc Condenses the Flow

  

The new procedure compresses the above into one set-based pipeline.

  

### Phase 1 - Resolve Builder and Candidate Names

  

The proc first resolves the builder's external organization id from `VeoSolutionsSecurity_organizations` / `VeoSolutionsSecurity_account_organizations`.

  

Then it builds candidate values for:

  

- community

- series

- plan

  

using the `VeoSolutionsSecurity` account-organization tables plus the mapping tables.

  

This preserves the existing behavior where both the builder-facing VDS name and the mapped Wisenbaker/Echelon name can be considered valid matches.

  

This is the same matching concept used by:

  

- `vds_selAccountOrganizationMatchingSpecCommunities`

- `vds_selAccountOrganizationMatchingSpecSeries`

- `vds_selUnmappedAccountOrganizationPlans`

  

However, instead of returning three separate result sets back to the application and then having `OptionPricingService` iterate over the combinations, the new proc materializes those candidate name sets as CTEs and keeps the resolution inside SQL.

  

### Phase 2 - Resolve Spec, Effective Date, and Plan

  

Once candidate names are known, the proc joins through Veo-side spec/plan metadata first:

  

- `Veo_spec_communities`

- `Veo_spec_series`

- `Veo_plan_mstr`

- `Veo_spec_mstr`

- `Veo_communities`

  

That narrows the search down to the relevant spec/plan combinations for the builder.

  

Then it joins to the WBS synonyms to determine the exact:

  

- active `spec_id`

- `prices_landed` `effective_date`

- `plan_id`

  

This replaces the older pattern where those were found in separate steps and separate procedures.

  

### Phase 3 - Replace Max/Min Build Cursor Logic with a Windowed Set

  

The old `vds_optionPricingMaxMinBuilds` proc uses a cursor to walk plan/build combinations and determine the representative build.

  

This new proc folds that behavior into CTEs:

  

- `build_candidates`

- `max_builds`

  

using a window-function / ranking approach.

  

For cabinets, the standard build is given priority when required.

  

For other cases, the highest field quantity wins.

  

This is a major simplification because the build-selection logic now lives inline with the rest of the price-level query instead of being a separate execution step.

  

### Phase 4 - Produce the Estimated Price Levels Directly

  

The `price_levels` CTE replaces the older `vds_selAllEstimatedOptionPricingItemsForNonSession_Yukon`-style intermediate result set.

  

It joins:

  

- `wbs_prices_landed`

- `wbs_plan_builds`

- `wbs_plan_mstr`

- `wbs_areas`

- `wbs_sub_areas`

- `wbs_applications`

- `wbs_products`

- `wbs_spec_areas_items`

- `wbs_areas_sub_areas`

- `wbs_builder_styles`

  

This produces the estimated price-level rows already decorated with:

  

- application name

- product name

- area/sub-area names

- builder style display name override when applicable

- correct price for the chosen build and effective date

  

### Phase 5 - Resolve Price Levels Down to Actual Parts

  

The most complicated part of the old flow was taking a price-level item and turning it into the actual part/color records that should appear in search results.

  

The new proc does this inline in the `resolved_colors` CTE using four paths:

  

1. `group -> styles_groups_detail(style) -> styles -> colors`

2. `group -> styles_groups_detail(color) -> colors`

3. `style -> colors`

4. `color -> direct part_no`

  

This is essentially the same conceptual resolution that used to be split across the Yukon group-level part logic, but now it is performed set-wise for the entire result set.

  

That means the proc resolves all candidate parts in one pass instead of repeatedly jumping out to additional procedure calls.

  

### Phase 6 - Builder Override and Final Search Filter

  

After the parts are resolved, the proc:

  

- applies color private-label overrides from `veo_colors_customer_overrides` when enabled

- filters to homebuyer-selectable parts via `Veo_stocking_codes`

- filters by search term against part name and part number

  

The final output is already in the exact shape needed by the caller.

  

## Why This Is Simpler Than the Old Way

  

The simplification is not that the underlying business rules disappeared.

  

The simplification is that they now live in one query pipeline instead of being spread across multiple stored procedures and multiple procedural hops.

  

More specifically:

  

- spec resolution is inline

- `prices_landed` effective-date resolution is inline

- `plan_id` resolution is inline

- max/default build selection is inline

- estimated price-level retrieval is inline

- group/style/color part resolution is inline

  

So instead of:

  

- call a proc to determine builds

- call another proc to get estimated price levels

- call another proc to resolve group-level parts

- stitch together results in application logic

  

we now do:

  

- one call

- one set-based query pipeline

- one final result set

  

## Practical Benefit

  

This reduces complexity in three ways:

  

1. **Fewer moving pieces**  

   The non-session estimated search path is easier to reason about because the full flow is visible in one stored procedure.

  

2. **Less procedural / N+1 behavior**  

   The windowed and CTE-based approach avoids repeated procedural round-trips and folds multiple sub-steps into one execution. That includes collapsing the legacy community/series/plan nested looping in `OptionPricingService` into one set-based name-resolution phase.

  

3. **Easier deployment and debugging**  

   When investigating pricing or part-resolution issues, there is now a single main proc to inspect rather than several interdependent Yukon helpers.

  

## Important Note

  

This new proc does not eliminate the business rules from the old solution.

  

It preserves them, but expresses them in a consolidated, set-based form that is much easier to deploy, reason about, and maintain.

✅QA Validation Completed! 

For your reference, you can see attached the video before and after the schema change to VEO_QA is deployed. 
All 26 products in dbo.Veo_QA.products are retrieved by GET https://qa.veodesignstudio.com/api/products and displayed under the 'Add Restricted Application Product' section. 

Additionally, you can see below that no alteration in the content of the results was found, as the API is retrieving the same 26 products both before and after. The only spotted difference is that they are not in the same order.

@Reid Wilson @Walter Martinez @Jennifer Hickey Thanks Reid! Just out of curiosity - I am hoping that triggers are not the issue? I ask because I think in VEO we have no triggers (since there are no write operations on the tables).

@Robert Hobbs @Walter Martinez @Jennifer Hickey

Hi, all. I have some definitive information to share with you.

The changes made to the products table by the Echelon team consist of constraint changes and trigger additions. For one reason or another, this was breaking DataSync.

To reproduce the DataSync issue, I can confirm that it was not working, syncing from WBS_Staging to VEO_DEV. VEO_DEV, like all of the other VEO databases, needs the schema updates described above. I have already deployed the schema changes to VEO_DEV. I then attempted a DataSync to see if the problem was fixed, and it was! So since the changes made it into DEV, I went ahead and came up with a simple testing plan, and tested it. The DEV environment passed.

Here is the testing plan I developed. It is rather simple yet effective.
  1. Navigate to VeoAdmin, and sign in.
  2. Navigate into the Details of any organization.
  3. Switch to the "Restricted Applications Products" tab.
  4. Act like you are about to add a restricted application product by hitting the related plus button. This launches a dialog.
  5. Select any application on the left-hand side.
Expected Result: All records present in the products table from VEO_X are loaded and displayed as products to choose from in the right-hand side of the dialog.

So with this in mind, I am going to wait to get a green light from QA regarding deploying schema changes for VEO_QA. Then, assuming the QA test passes, we can deploy to the production databases.

there are 2 pages added to the WIKI under "Data Sync and SQL Compare" that have directions and video links for Adrian's Data Sync app and directions for Redgate's SQL Compare.
https://dev.azure.com/BuildOnTechnologies/VeoDesignStudio/_wiki/wikis/VeoDesignStudio/2650/Data-Sync-and-Sql-Compare

• sync data structure between production Echelon (WBS) and wbs_staging 
  • run compare for production Echelon (WBS) and VEO
    • determine if the changes are similar or wildly different (document) 
  • sync data structure between wbs_staging and VEO_[environments] 
    • for each difference, determine whether the change should be made (is it relevant), and what the impact might be 
    • for each change made determine if testing needs to happen in the app to ensure there are no regressions 
      • itemize areas to retest based on changes 
  • create a script we can run to apply changes to VEO production 
    • includes VEO, VEO_EPP, VEO_AFI, VEO_CCDI

Each of the following, and their derived tints and shades are editable by the user.  Each one should have a main color, shown below, (no suffix), 3 derived tints, and 3 derived shades.
  • --palette-primary
  • --palette-secondary
  • --palette-tertiary
  • --palette-neutral
Any variables in the sections prefixed with  '//semantic...', in the code comments, are the non-editable 'system' colors.
  • --pallete-success
  • --palette-warning
  • --palette-error
There are 2 'semantic' (system) colors that (ideally) represent absolute dark and absolute light, they are as follows, respectively...
  • --semantic-negative
  • --semantic-positive
The '// gradients' section contains variables with baked-in linear gradient definitions, using palette color tints and shades in their color slots.  These will not be directly editable by the user, but only indirectly editable, via changing the main palette color slots and their derived tints and shades.  They are...
  • --palette-gradient-neutral-light
  • --palette-gradient-neutral
  • --palette-gradient-neutral-dark
  • --palette-gradient-primary-light
  • --palette-gradient-primary
  • --palette-gradient-primary-dark
  • --palette-gradient-secondary-light
  • --palette-gradient-secondary
  • --palette-gradient-secondary-dark

@[VeoDesignStudio]\VeoDesignStudio Team

This issue has happened now twice. The plan area for the particular builds (Floor Wood, Floor Vinyl Plank, Floor Tile in this example) did not match on sub_area_id. In VDS, this can be releaved on our end within the GetRoomOptions useCase:
lines 110-137
            _roomOptions = builds
                .Where(b => !b.IsCredit && !b.IsAllStandardAreaBuild && b.RoomId != null)
                .GroupBy(b => new { b.RoomName, b.RoomId, b.IconId, b.ApplicationId, b.Application, b.ProductId, b.Product, b.AreaId, b.SubAreaId })
                .Select(g => new GetRoomOptionsUC_RoomOptionDTO
                {
                    RoomName = g.Key.RoomName,
                    RoomId = (Guid)g.Key.RoomId,
                    IconId = g.Key.IconId,
                    Application = g.Key.Application,
                    ApplicationId = g.Key.ApplicationId,
                    Product = g.Key.Product,
                    ProductId = g.Key.ProductId,
                    IsStandard = g.All(b => b.IsStandardProduct),
                    HasFreePriceLevel = g.Any(b => b.HasFreePriceLevel),
                    Selection = selections
                        .Where(s => s.SessionId == _sessionId && s.RoomId == (Guid)g.Key.RoomId && s.ApplicationId == g.Key.ApplicationId && s.ProductId == g.Key.ProductId && s.SubAreaId == g.Key.SubAreaId)
                        .GroupBy(s => new { s.SelectedOptionId, s.SelectedOptionName })
                        .Select(sg => new GetRoomOptionsUC_RoomOptionSelectionDTO
                        {
                            ItemId = sg.Key.SelectedOptionId,
                            ItemDescription = sg.Key.SelectedOptionName,
                            Total = sg.Sum(i => i.SelectedOptionPrice)
                        })
                        .FirstOrDefault(),
                    AreaId = g.Key.AreaId,
                    SubAreaName = g.FirstOrDefault().BuildDescription,
                    SubAreaId = g.Key.SubAreaId
                }).ToList();

How to Use
1.  Navigate to Organizations → Organizations
2.  Select a Builder from the Organizations List
3.  Click the Report Options tab, within the View Organization screen for the builder selected to view Selections Report Options (titled "Report Options")
4.  Toggle options ON/OFF and ENABLED/DISABLED as needed
5.  Save => Confirm to persist changes or Cancel (prior to clicking initial Save button) to revert

Selections Report Options — Feature Summary Branch: devs/28419/selections_report_options_veo_admin Location: VEO Admin → Organizations → Report Options --- Overview New view allowing users to configure Selections Report Options for a Builder (Account + Organization combination). Users select a Builder from a dropdown, then view and edit report options in a grid. --- How to Use 1. Navigate to Organizations → Report Options 2. Select a Builder from the dropdown 3. Grid displays report options for that Builder 4. Toggle options ON/OFF and ENABLED/DISABLED as needed 5. Save to persist changes or Cancel to revert --- Defaults - Defaults are established at the application level - "Default" indicator displays when a value matches the application-level default - Builders without prior configuration display default options - Cancel reverts changes back to the original/default values --- Constraints - Designer Documents and Catalog Documents are read-only (cannot be edited) --- Validation Rules | Condition | Result | |--------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------| | Legacy Report = ON + Condensed Format = ON | Invalid — error message displayed | | Legacy Report = ON + GroupBy: Areas | Invalid — error message displayed | Behavior: - Save/Cancel buttons are hidden until UI is in a valid state - Error messages match backend 400 response text for consistency - User must resolve the conflict before saving is allowed --- Save Button Error State Scenario: User makes changes (Save/Cancel appears), but then sets incompatible options. The conflicting rows may be scrolled out of view, leaving the user confused about why Save is disabled. Solution: Display a simple error message under the Save button (e.g., "Incompatible settings") to alert the user that there's an issue somewhere in the grid. Behavior: - Save/Cancel buttons appear when any option is dirty - Save button disables when incompatible options are selected - Error message displays below Save button: "Incompatible settings" - User scrolls to find and fix the row-level error - Once resolved, error clears and Save enables --- UI Standards | Item | Decision | |-------------------|----------------------------------------------------------| | Button casing | Mixed case (e.g., "On", "Off", "Enabled") — not all caps | | Default indicator | "Default" (full word, not abbreviated) | | View name | "Report Options" | --- Testing Scenarios | Scenario | Expected Result | |--------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------| | Select a Builder | Grid loads report options for that Builder | | Switch to different Builder | Grid refreshes with new Builder's options | | Toggle an option → Save | Change persists after page refresh | | Toggle an option → Cancel | Reverts to original/default value | | Option matches app default | Shows "Default" indicator | | Option differs from default | No "Default" indicator | | Set Legacy Report ON + Condensed Format ON | Row error shown; Save disabled; "Incompatible settings" under Save | | Set Legacy Report ON + GroupBy: Areas | Row error shown; Save disabled; "Incompatible settings" under Save | | Scroll away from error row | "Incompatible settings" message remains visible under Save | | Fix invalid combination | Error clears; Save enables | | Resize browser window | Grid scrolls internally; no second page scrollbar | | Edit Designer/Catalog Documents | Should be disabled/non-editable | --- Screenshots
  **Screenshot 1: Row validation error**



  **Screenshot 2: Save button disabled state**

18327.text: @Robert Hobbs, @Jennifer Hickey, @Walter Martinez, @Victor Monterrey Marking this work item "DONE" per our conversation in standup this morning.

@Robert Hobbs, @Jim Warnement  I have not found the source of the issue yet, but I did notice that with 'Sterlina II matte 12x24 Silver' and many other patterns other than 'Vertical Off-set Stack' (I did not test every single other pattern, but I did test at least 4 or 5 others) the issue did not occur.

Also, the issue does occur with other tiles (tested various sized, for example, 'Catch Gloss 2x8 - Silicone', 'Larchmont 6x35 - Rue', 'Porto II Polished 24x24 - Fawn') using the same pattern 'Vertical Off-set Stack'.

I also tested a price level 2 tile, with the 'Vertical Off-set Stack' pattern and the issue did occur.

These results seem to suggest some sort of issue with that specific pattern.  I could be wrong, however, and if it is the pattern, I don't yet know what about that pattern might be causing the issue.

22160.text: @Daniel Arwe @Jennifer Hickey @Walter Martinez The updated colors look good.

@Jennifer Hickey, @Walter Martinez Documenting fixes here:

Also fixed hover and active button styles here as well, just not shown in screenshots.  The fix was to change the value of these variables, which took care of all secondary buttons throughout the app, changing them from gray to shades of a pale blue found on Stylecraft's website (which were then adjusted 20-30% darker, via HSL value change to the lightness value).  Cleared this color with @Charlie Bradley III.






As mentioned in an earlier discussion post, the trash can and pencil icons, on HB summary session card, and 'Impersonating {user}' respectively, had to be left as is, since they derive from the variable value of color-icon-primary.  Changing that value would have sweeping, and unintended effects to almost every icon in the app.  I spoke with @Jennifer Hickey and she agreed that, in cases like this, concessions may have to be made to the 'no gray, active buttons/links' web design convention.

Notes to self:

Changes to be made, both those pointed out and a few others that will just look better.  Care was taken to not change variables with wide-reaching affects, with the exception of btn-secondary...

That was the prior functionality, but the expected functionality is that you would be able to log in just fine with your target user and target organization and see the designer dashboard and be able to switch to any eligible organization easily!

@Daniel Arwe @Walter Martinez @Robert Hobbs @Joe Ebeling
I'm concerned about our ability to meet our sprint commitment and wanted to outline my thoughts related to this story and Daniel's story. I've also added a note to Daniel's story as we have scope creep on 2 stories within the sprint.

This story has scope creep. We tend to only think of scope creep as something developers have to manage to prevent their development efforts from being derailed, but scope creep is an unexpected and added burden to QA's function as well. Scope creep impacts development (coding and testing) time needed for a story and introduces risk in a story not finishing primary development. We fail to recongize that scope creep, weather requested by QA, PO or dev, also impacts the testing effort. These additional efforts are not accounted for in the original score. Just as developer protect their ability to complete user stories, QA should also ensure the team's sprint commitment can be met and evaluate our ability to accommodate scope creep within a sprint. There is no question that we all want to deliver value in a timely fashion. Scope creep happens and can be valuable when accommodated for. My goal here is to bring our attention to the collective impacts of scope creep on the whole team.

In this user story, including Missing Images from the Details tab to the new functionality of this story is scope creep. When scoring QA did not account for regression testing Missing Image and we do not have AC to document the expectation. (@Justin Pope can add these AC once decided upon/implemented.) QA is not expecting issues related to regression testing the missing image, but the necessirty of this does add risk to this user story. The additional regression testing also introduced greater risk for this story returning to In Dev with issues found.

Another concideration is that this story has scope creep that necessitates unexpected regression testing and Daniel's story has scope creep. As a team, we do not know if QA has capacity for this additional testing effort across both stories and we do not know how it will effect later stories in the sprint. I am by no means saying the scope creep here or on the other story is not needed or desired. Moving the Missing Image functionality makes sense, but I do want the team to be aware if the implecations and risk.

@Jennifer Hickey @Walter Martinez @Joe Ebeling @Robert Hobbs @Daniel Arwe @Reid Wilson @Charlie Bradley III

30828.text:
✅QA Validation Completed! 

PFA a quick screenshare of one of my tests. 
Followed the steps to reproduce above.

@Robert Hobbs 

More details. Do with these as you wish.

High-level details maybe more suitable for release notes:
The logic to determine when to display the error message shown below was not strict enough, leading to a few edge cases where the message would be seen incorrectly. The logic has been made more strict, so that the message is only shown when the impersonator is actually restricted from viewing the buyer's default profile plan due to assigned community rules, as the message states.

Deeper details:
The logic to check whether to show the message or not was failing to check if there is actually an impersonated user in the first place. It sees that the local storage value is true and tries to enact it. It sees that buyer experience cannot actually be turned on, and throws the error message. But the real reason it can't be turned on is not because of assigned communities rules. It is because there is no impersonated user to begin with.
@Reid Wilson seems good. I tried both paths and also just did random things in the app and didn't see the message. Any details on the fix here would be helpful to my release notes.

Another repro path:

1) Setup: find or create a homebuyer with a profile plan, call it homebuyer A
2) Impersonate homebuyer A.
3) Turn buyer experience ON.
4) Log out. (this leaves buyer experience local storage toggle as true)
5) Log in as homebuyer A.
6) Enter Option Pricing or DMH.
7) Hit "Home".

Expected Result: The user should be taken to the landing page. No weird dialogue message is displayed.
Actual Result: The user is correctly taken to the landing page. However, the weird dialogue message is incorrectly displayed.

@[VeoDesignStudio]\VeoDesignStudio Team

Just wanted to make sure every one is aware of an implementation detail. This was alluded to in previous comments but I am making a stab to solidify what was done here.

Previous Functionality:
The allowed categories fetched on the following:
  • All applications from Echelon (via our Veo db)
  • All applications from VDS Catalog items
  • if the builders catalog source is set to VCMS, include all top level catagories (this is every single one)
Short comings:
1) Out of the box, the endpoint we were using did not expose the properties of the categories that VCMS returned thus, we wouldn't know if a category was a stub for Enhanced Categories.
1 solution) To solve this, we ware going to get the builders specific categories and only look at the "top" level categories and not sub categories.
This fell in line with how we were grabbing applications previously. We also are only seeing the categories for the builder and not every category across the whole application.

2) If a builder has a VCMS catalog, it may be confusing or unintuitive about where the category is coming from.
2 solution) We only use the catalog provider that the builder is set with to fetch the categories with the right catalog
This was to solve not fetching VDS catalog item applications when they are set with VCMS catalog.

More information from today:

So, I went in deep.

I went to the Echelon sql server and looked at  [Echelon-EPP].[dbo].[style_attributes] there. Jim Warnement, what I am seeing there is that you haven't made changes since august of 2023. I modified the styles (TIMNEW5,TIMNEW5C,TIMNEW5D,TIMNEW5P) from 'New Haven 5 Piece' to 'New Haven 5 Piece (TL)'.

I made the same modification to [EPLAN_VEO].[dbo].[style_attributes]. NOTE: the audit fields on the records I touched the other day still showed the same modification date but the value_id did roll back. I believe the datasync is not touching the audit fields / rely on the triggers that we do not have on our side.

 

Went into the session that was showing the issue with Jim Warnement and it is fixed.

Ultimately, the issue is that two particular styles (TIMBEW5, TIMNEW5P) had incorrect style attributes for door styles that did not match up to the correct cabinet door style.
When that was corrected the colors were coming in correct.

@Daniel Arwe Yes, though lamentable, I think we should create an override for existing legacy themes that adds a color that matches that is in production now for that theme. We want these changes to be deliberate when we refactor those themes.

@<6E668580-F8AB-64C3-A4C1-1DDE45503DA2> , @<16695B85-2F2C-6860-B106-056F5D5396E1> , @<4C46EFE8-26F9-6F48-B257-971123DDB984>.....

As you can see below, the card price text looks the same as it did for the 4 test themes (as expected, since this element was previously pointed at --text-primary-alt1 and the new variable --color-optionPricing-selectionSummary-card-price now also points at --text-primary-alt1, unless it is changed, for a given theme to point to something else.

However, the change to variablize the background of the card (using the variable --bg-optionPricing-selectionSummary-card) will affect the appearance of some themes in production.  This is unavoidable and a consequence of changing the card background from what was previously a semi-opaque layer to what is now a solid color, which is a change that needed to happen to allow themeability for this attribute of the card.  It does mean that, since this was previously an unvariablized element attribute (previously hard-coded) that, if deployed as-is, all production themes not using the skeleton (which is pretty much all of them) will fall back to the VDS (Custom) theme for that card's background color.  Again, this is as expected and not an error or oversight.

**Therefore, my question is....Do we own the responsibility of making a script that will insert a ThemeableVariableValue record overriding the card background color to something more asthetically in line with each theme's color scheme, or does that responsibility fall to Service and Support, leaving our responsibility only being to notify them to check each production theme to verify the card looks how they want and change that variable's value if it doesn't?**

✅QA Validation Completed! 

Validate that the old dashboard toggle for the homebuyer is not visible when 'Disallow HB Access' flag is ON and 'Buyer experience' flag is OFF and when the 'Buyer experience' is ON. 



Validate that when both flags are off is the only combination that will show the old toggle.

@Walter Martinez

Hi Walter! Sorry you missed the pre-story meeting. Rob and I ended up doing it without you. But I am updating you on everything here. In fact, there is not much to update you on anyways considering the small size of this story.

The use case and AC seem very straightforward. We could not envision any edge cases that would cause us to deviate from the AC. One thing not explicitly mentioned in the story is the existing logic to determine the visibility of this legacy toggle switch. That existing logic is this: it will appear if the logged in user is impersonating a homebuyer and the buyer experience flag is set to OFF. Otherwise, it will not appear. But we are changing it to function like it says in the AC (which just means that we are adding a criteria that the disallow HB access feature flag has to be OFF for the element to be visible).

As always, please reach out with any questions or concerns you may have, and I will answer them as promptly as possible. Thanks.

23159.text:
Searching all refrences within our SQL Servers of DEV and PROD
RM00101 and RM00201 results.xlsx

SQL Evaluation of RM00101 and RM00201

My results line up with Rob's searching for the story.

Within the VeoSolutions database, the following objects refrence RM00101:

procedure: vs_selCustomerClass

procedure: vds_selSpecCustomEdgeItems

procedure: vds_selSpecEdgeItems

synonym: veo_rm00101

 

There were no references to RM00201 to be found in VeoSolutions database.

Within VeoSolutionsSecurity database, there were no references to either table.

 

Evaluation of each SQL object:

 

proc vs_selCustomerClass:

  • The sql file is within the repository for the procedure, but there is no reference to execute this procedure within SSMS or the C# code for our back end.
  • Recommend removal of this procedure.
  • NOTE: the procedure takes a session_id and returns the custclas off the rm00101 record. The joins go from organizations to rm00101 joining on organizations.external_organization_id to rm00101.custnmbr which is very much wrong.

proc vds_selSpecCustomEdgeItems

  • this procedure lives on the data base but there is no SQL file within the code base and there is no reference to execute this procedure wihtin SSMS or the C# code for our back end.
  • Recommend removal of this procedure.

 

proc vds_selSpecEdgeItems

  • this procedure has a SQL file within the code base and is referenced to execute within our C# backend.
  • the utilization of rm00101 is to get custclas and set a variable, but the procedure never does anything with the @cust_class variable.
  • Within the select statement, it is just a left join to rm00101.
  • Removal of rm00101 and the @cust_class variable is needed

    Utilization
  • This procedure is utilized in getting BOM items for a Build when the item is an edge item

synonym veo_rm00101

  • removal of this synonym is required.
@[VeoDesignStudio]\VeoDesignStudio Team In the WIKI, the bottom item is "Data Sync and Sql Compare" and has 2 files under it. They go through the steps to sync data and schema.

@Jennifer Hickey, @Walter Martinez, @Victor Monterrey

I noticed your questions on the 2 test cases and thought I would try to answer them here.  Please let me know if you need further clarification....

I am in the process of gaining access to Azure Service Bus message queue.  I have a meeting with Andy in the morning to go over this, at which time I will provide additional information on how to check messages received there.

The DomainEventingEnabled flag here is the flag referred to the in AC.  It is not referring to a feature flag.  To toggle it on and off, you'll need to go into Pipelines > Library > VDS QA and change it there for each test (ON & OFF).



For the Integration application message queue http://integration-dev.buildontechnologies.com/system/vds/messages, this is what you should see on session completion (be sure to set filters on the left as shown):
@Daniel Arwe yep seems like we should log it. I would do it from the backend if that's the better approach code-wise.

@Robert Hobbs After a bit more digging, I learned that the session, status_change event wasn't getting logged when changing the session status from the Homebuyer Summary here....



It only gets logged when changing session status via this UI path:



In my opinion, the best fix for this would be to log the event for a session status change from the backend, since both locations in the frontend application call the same endpoint to set the session status.  However, this will likely add a few more hours to the story (hopefully, I could implement this change by EOD today or early tomorrow at the latest).  A bandaid approach would be to call the existing frontend method from the other frontend location (Homebuyer Summary) where it is currently not being called, to log the status change....followed by a new story to be worked on in the future to move the logic to a backend implementation.  How do you want me to proceed?

@<3591AA25-65FB-6983-B241-7CF16106F563> @<6E668580-F8AB-64C3-A4C1-1DDE45503DA2> @<741B2728-CE5D-6535-931E-5A652D2CDD41> 
What if we also include "StatusName" or "StatusDescription" that could contain something human-friendly?

otherwise, let's see how it looks in the event log and see if it has enough information.

@Eric Hickey @Robert Hobbs As far as the VDS Event Log event recorded, we already have the following event type...

category: 'session',
action: 'status_change',


I don't love the fact that we are logging a number for StatusID, rather than a string ('active', 'complete', etc.), due to reduced human-readability, but that is the structure as it is defined currently.  Will this existing event suffice for recording the session completion event as it pertains to the requirements of this story, or do we need a new event?

@<741B2728-CE5D-6535-931E-5A652D2CDD41> @<3591AA25-65FB-6983-B241-7CF16106F563> @<6E668580-F8AB-64C3-A4C1-1DDE45503DA2> 
I very likely did discuss it and I totally forgot (I should have updated the text above).  We can skip it and deal with it at some point in the future.

@Daniel Arwe @Eric Hickey @Robert Hobbs I thought we discussed this during sizing and decided against adding this feature. We spoke a lot about what makes a message unique. I don't think adding an extra identifier would be beneficial now. If we are adding that concept, I would like it to be more fully baked into the full integration system. Should we have a conversation?

@Robert Hobbs, It looks like, currently, we don't have a specific event recorded in the VDS Event Log on session completion.  Closest thing we have is an event for the selections report auto-generation....



If we add another event, what category and action strings do you want logged, and what do you want in the JSON data?

@Eric Hickey, to answer your concern about size implications (assuming you mean time and effort added to the story), I don't anticipate this change adding many points to story.  It's already a 5 and so far I am on pace to come in well under that threshold.

@Eric Hickey @Andrew Fry @Robert Hobbs Andy, I assume this would require a new verson of the domain eventing package to be pushed to the Nuget feed, implementing this additional property on the SessionCompletedEvent class, correct?  If that's true, how long would that take to get implemented and pushed out?

So far, I have VDS code changes in place that fulfill all of the requirements of the AC except that EventId.  End of the current sprint for VDS is Tuesday, November 19, but QA would need a bit of buffer to get the story tested before we release it.

@<3591AA25-65FB-6983-B241-7CF16106F563> @<741B2728-CE5D-6535-931E-5A652D2CDD41> @<6E668580-F8AB-64C3-A4C1-1DDE45503DA2> 
If we do this, VDS would be the party responsible for creating the event Id.

Here's the conceptual idea:
If a session is completed, and vds delivers a message "somewhere".  If that message fails, and a copy of the message exists "somewhere", we could resubmit it.  However, technically we could resubmit it 1000 times.  The event Id would provide the ability to track if that particular message has already been processed, although nothing exists today to leverage it.  This event id is intended to uniquely represent _this particular event_.  Logically: This moment in time where a user has completed this session.

It could be a Guid, and I can't think of anything better at the moment.  I have a small concern that we are creating an Id that is immediately discarded by the creator, but that may be what it has to be right now.  I would call it **Event Id**, and I would put it in the payload; not as an additional argument to the SendSessionCompletedEvent method.

@Andrew Fry, @Eric Hickey, @Robert Hobbs
For the EPH NOTES highlighted in the AC, how do we want to implement this?  I was thinking this could happen one of a few ways:

  1.  Change the SessionCompletedEvent to include another property (maybe 'MessageSendEventId' (GUID)?), allowing VDS to generate and set it on the SessionCompletedEvent that it passes to SendSessionCompletedEvent OR pass another parameter (messageSendEventId)  to SendSessionCompletedEvent for the eventing package code to handle internally and attach to each event, in both destinations.

24239.text:
@Robert Hobbs

Help with CSS filters for themeable variables:

If you see a themeable variable with a CSS name that starts with "filter", as in "filter-dmh-roomProductIcon", that is a strong sign that a regular hex code, as in "#0000ff" will not work. You must use what is called a CSS filter. Here is an example of converting a hex code color to CSS filter:

"#0000ff" is full blue in hex code
"brightness(0) saturate(100%) invert(9%) sepia(79%) saturate(7467%) hue-rotate(247deg) brightness(101%) contrast(147%)" is the same color but in CSS filter format

Obviously, CSS filter format is not self-explanatory. However, you can visit the following link to easily convert from an easy color definition, like RGB or hex code, to a CSS filter.

https://angel-rs.github.io/css-color-filter-generator/

@Robert Hobbs

You requested some themeable variable location information. I have that here for you.

OP: We do have an existing variable solely for the background of the left nav panel: var(--bg-optionPricing-leftPane)
DS: We do have an existing variable solely for the background of the left nav panel: var(--bg-ds-leftPane)
DMH: We do have an existing variable for the background of the left nav panel regardless of general options selector, room option selector, or full catalog selector: var(--bg-dmh-leftPanel-nav)

✅QA Validation Completed!
For customer made theme. 





And for VDS Default theme

Implementation Notes

Analysis of current app state for text variablization of different nav menus:
  • DS: no variable at all
  • DMH
    • var(--color-dmh-leftNav-listItemText) in general option selector; this variable is used nowhere else
    • var(--color-dmh-icons-light-on-darkBg) in room option selector; this variable is used elsewhere
    • var(--color-dmh-icons-light-on-darkBg) in full catalog selector; this variable is used elsewhere
  • OP: no variable at all
Technical changes made:
  • DS: new variable: var(--color-ds-navList-text); points by default at palette slot var(--semantic-positive) which is locked in as #ffffff (white)
  • DMH
    • keep the nav menu in general option selector as is (still using var(--color-dmh-leftNav-listItemText)); however, instead of pointing by default at palette slot var(--palette-neutral-light-3) which defaults to #fafafa, this variable now points by default at palette slot var(--semantic-positve) which is locked in as #fffff (white) (the difference is indistinguishable)
    • change variable in room option selector to var(--color-dmh-leftNav-listItemText)
    • change variable in full catalog selector to var(--color-dmh-leftNav-listItemText)
  • OP: new variable: var(--color-optionPricing-appProductList-text); points by default at palette slot var(--semantic-positive) which is locked in as #ffffff (white)

In simple terms, if you do not override any relevant variables or select any relevant palette slots for a theme, the text color in all nav menus will appear white by default.

Doofenshmirtz Investigation
The reason that the Doofenshmirtz inspector says "No variables found." sometimes when you think there should be is that the inspector is technically correct. No variables are found on the target element. But with the way CSS works, the target element can read certain CSS properties, including the color property, from its parent. So in the case of the DMH left nav menu (at least in room option selector), no variable is applied to the text itself, but it reads a variable from the color property of a parent element.

29468.text:
@<6E668580-F8AB-64C3-A4C1-1DDE45503DA2> 
I performed an experiment to test the performance benefits of returning a smaller image-size pixel-wise (no rhyming intented :-) ).  

I used a .NET minimal API and several test images, ranging from 4,000px X 4,000px (large and size currently returned by BioRev's createScene endpoint, which get ths render), 2,000px X 2,000px (medium) and 1,000px X 1,000px (small), returning them via base64 in the JSON response and as straight up binary delivery (both 'webp' format).  I recorded the results across 10 trials, via Postman, for each scenario (large, medium, small & base64 vs binary).  I averaged the response time for each scenario, using the large image, delivered via base64 (the current method by which BioRev returns the image) locally as the baseline, since using the actual BioRev return times would not be a fair apples to apples comparison (due to the fact that BioRev's API is getting hit on a remote server, localhost is much faster, simply due to transport distance factors).  **My results are linked in the attachments.** ([image_benchmark_trials.xlsx](https://dev.azure.com/BuildOnTechnologies/ddde192e-e31e-4e51-af4b-3b7b4221266a/_apis/wit/attachments/4a62f507-5a2f-4c3c-a8ae-f7b25ffce4f2?fileName=image_benchmark_trials.xlsx&download=true))

The data makes it clear that image size is the primary driver of performance. Returning smaller, right-sized images yields the largest gains, even without changing the response format, and binary delivery provides an additional but secondary improvement.
I have saved some logging that I have captured. My analysis does not reveal much that we can do to optimize this process. Most of the time is sunk into getting the render from BioRev.

with biorev we are making a call for the scene configuration every time 
  • add metrics for the calls 
  • re-evaluate the call and identify what needs to be modified 
  • cache the get options call so we aren't making that more than once 
  • for any render we go to our back end and collate all selections get those packaged up and then go to the visualizer provider and then to the client again
